target.title: feat(msteams): add native plugin interactivity parity
target.body:
## Summary

DO NOT MERGE YET -- for review

- Problem: OpenClaw's Teams channel could send messages/cards, but the native plugin interactivity path still stopped short of Teams. The Codex app-server Teams work therefore had to rely on a command bridge instead of the same core plugin/runtime flow used by first-class channels.
- Why it matters: I am working on this so Teams support for the Codex app-server path is grounded in real OpenClaw channel support, not a partial workaround. Without these core changes, Teams actions, binding flows, message edits, and channel-scoped Graph operations do not have the same native foundation as Discord/Telegram-style plugin integrations.
- What changed: this PR adds native Teams plugin interactive dispatch in `src/plugins/interactive.ts` and `src/plugins/interactive-dispatch-adapters.ts`, adds the Teams runtime surface in `src/plugins/runtime/runtime-msteams.ts`, `src/plugins/runtime/runtime-msteams-typing.ts`, and `src/plugins/runtime/runtime-msteams-ops.runtime.ts`, wires Teams invoke handling through `extensions/msteams/src/plugin-interactions.ts` and `extensions/msteams/src/monitor-handler.ts`, extends command/target normalization in `src/plugins/commands.ts` and `src/channels/plugins/target-parsing.ts`, and fixes Teams Graph/edit/allowlist correctness in `extensions/msteams/src/send.ts`, `extensions/msteams/src/graph.ts`, `extensions/msteams/src/graph-messages.ts`, `extensions/msteams/src/graph-thread.ts`, `extensions/msteams/src/resolve-allowlist.ts`, and `extensions/msteams/src/policy.ts`.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): this does not try to fix unrelated latest-`main` agent/skills TypeScript failures, and it does not claim live-tenant validation for every Teams topology (for example shared-channel behavior still needs real-environment confirmation outside this PR).

## Change Type

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [x] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [x] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope

That left Codex app-server Teams support depending on a hidden command bridge instead of the same first-class interactive surface exposed to other channels.
- Missing detection / guardrail: there was no Teams-native seam/integration coverage for plugin invoke handling, source-message edits/clears, Graph channel id translation, or fail-closed allowlist resolution.

- Why this regressed now: the cross-repo Codex Teams work pushed beyond that MVP and exposed the remaining OpenClaw core gaps.

## Regression Test Plan

- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the failures crossed the Teams adapter, plugin interactive core, and Graph-backed Teams operations; narrower unit tests would miss the integration seams that were actually broken.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): the files above now cover the main Teams seams.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- Teams plugin actions now go through native OpenClaw plugin interactivity instead of relying on a command-only bridge.
- Teams plugin replies/follow-ups/edit-source/clear-actions now preserve better source-message behavior for Adaptive Card flows.
- Teams plugin command binding and explicit target parsing now normalize `teams:` / `msteams:` conversation targets consistently.
- Teams channel-scoped Graph operations use the resolved Graph team id path more safely.
- Teams allowlist resolution now fails closed when primary-channel lookup cannot safely resolve a usable runtime key.

## Diagram

```text
Before:
Teams Action.Submit -> Teams-specific command bridge -> plugin workaround -> partial updates

After:
Teams Action.Submit -> extensions/msteams/src/plugin-interactions.ts

```

The risk is widening what a plugin can do in Teams or routing to the wrong target. Mitigations in this PR are explicit runtime typing in `src/plugins/runtime/types-channel.ts`, targeted runtime wiring instead of broad generic exposure, conversation-scoped interactive handling in `extensions/msteams/src/plugin-interactions.ts`, Graph team-id resolution fixes in `extensions/msteams/src/graph-thread.ts` / `extensions/msteams/src/graph.ts`, and fail-closed allowlist behavior in `extensions/msteams/src/resolve-allowlist.ts` / `extensions/msteams/src/policy.ts`.

target.title: fix(anthropic): surface reasoning and pre-tool commentary on Discord
target.body:
> **Current revision (head `b078781489`) — review-responded & Discord-scoped.** Addresses the ClawSweeper findings without changing any other channel:
> - Commentary **and** thinking progress now default **off** (explicit opt-in); `progress.thinking` is contract-wired into the type, zod schema, **and** the generated channel-config metadata (validates at config-load).
> - Message-tool-only reasoning-privacy invariant **restored** (trailing reasoning suppressed uniformly); opaque tool-call id keying restored.
> - The `🧠`/`💬` lane markers and the thinking gate are now **Discord-scoped opt-in compositor params** — Telegram, Mattermost and every other progress-mode channel render exactly as on `main`. This PR touches no other channel.
> - Verified live on Discord (`anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6`), plain (`_p_`) + verbose/reasoning (`_rv_`). See the [review-response comment](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/96106#issuecomment-4797749572) for the per-finding walkthrough.

---

## What Problem This Solves

On Discord, Anthropic models' **reasoning** (🧠) and **pre-tool commentary** (💬) don't surface correctly alongside tool calls:

- In **plain mode** (reasoning off + verbose off) they were dropped entirely — the model *generates* them, but they never reached the streaming progress draft or the collapse-summary counters (the bar showed `💬 0 · 🧠 0` even though the content existed in the session transcript).
- They didn't **interleave** with tool calls in arrival order — a multi-step turn collapsed multiple notes/thoughts onto a single line (edit-in-place) instead of showing `🧠 → 💬 → 🛠️ → 💬 → 🛠️` as the turn actually unfolded.

## Why This Change Was Made

`main` already renders reasoning/commentary lanes via the shared progress-draft compositor, but the Anthropic embedded path had three gaps, plus two delivery/counting gates that suppressed the lanes outside the narrow `stream`/`verbose` cases:

1. Anthropic tool-turn narration isn't tagged as commentary, so it's never recognized/routed.
2. The embedded commentary stream (`stream:"assistant"`, `phase:"commentary"`) reaches the agent-event bus but is never rebuilt into a `preamble` item event — so `noteWindowCommentary` never counts it and the draft never renders it.
3. Window thinking was gated on `reasoningMode === "stream"`, so plain mode (reasoning *off*) dropped reasoning from the window draft and counters. Window thinking is *presentation* — it should stream for any mode except `"on"` (which uses durable 🧠 persistence instead).

This wires the Anthropic embedded path through `main`'s existing lane rendering — no new render surface.

## User Impact

Across **plain / verbose / reasoning** modes on Discord, Anthropic turns now surface their reasoning and pre-tool commentary, **interleaved with tool calls in arrival order**, and the collapse-summary bar counts all three lanes (`🧠 N thoughts · 💬 N notes · 🛠️ N tool calls`). No config changes; no new render surface.

## Evidence

Live, on a build carrying these commits (Discord, `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6`), verified by reading the bot's actual channel messages:

**Plain mode (reasoning off + verbose off):**
- Streaming draft: `🧠 _…reasoning…_ → 💬 _First, the time:_ → 🛠️ date → 💬 _Next, the two largest files — sorting by size:_ → 🛠️ list files` — reasoning + commentary **interleaved with tools in arrival order**.
- Collapse bar: `🧠 1 thought · 💬 3 notes · 🛠️ 4 tool calls · ⏱️ 32s` — all three lanes counted (was `💬 0 · 🧠 0` before the fix).

**Verbose + reasoning mode:** commentary renders as durable messages interleaved with tools: `💬 → 🛠️ → 💬 → 🛠️ → 💬 → 🛠️`. No regression.

**After-fix evidence (plain mode, reasoning off + verbose off):**

Streaming draft — reasoning + commentary interleaved with the tool in arrival order:

![plain streaming interleave](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Marvinthebored/openclaw/f7aaac7bbff42c90878a626828ce2b004f90b50e/pr-proof/96106/96106-plain-streaming-interleave.png)

Collapse summary — all three lanes counted:

![plain collapse counts](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Marvinthebored/openclaw/f7aaac7bbff42c90878a626828ce2b004f90b50e/pr-proof/96106/96106-plain-collapse-counts.png)


**Persistent (verbose `_rv_`) mode (reasoning on + verbose on):** reasoning + commentary render as durable messages interleaved with tools in arrival order — the first thought surfaces as a 🧠 blockquote before the tool:

![rv persistent interleave](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Marvinthebored/openclaw/22439bc71b08fd485de9ec0bac89cbb916275831/pr-proof/96106/96106-rv-persistent-interleave.png)

Validation: `tsgo` (core + test-types) pass; lane suites green (`message-handler.process.test.ts`, `embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.messages.test.ts`, `progress-draft-compositor.test.ts` including the `"interleaves reasoning bursts with tool calls in arrival order"` assertion); `build-all` exit 0.

## Linked context

- **Fixes #96079.**
- **Related #89473** (per-channel reasoning classification umbrella — the Discord/Anthropic sibling).

target.title: fix(status): use legacy Codex OAuth profiles for OpenAI usage
target.body:
## Summary
- map legacy `openai-codex` OAuth/token profiles to the canonical `openai` usage provider
- let provider usage auth resolution try `openai-codex:*` credentials when collecting `openai` ChatGPT usage, including plugins that explicitly request `resolveOAuthToken({ provider: "openai" })`
- keep API keys excluded from ChatGPT usage auth

## Context
After the OpenAI/Codex provider migration, a user can still have a healthy legacy `openai-codex:*` OAuth profile while `openclaw status --usage` now asks the usage collector for provider `openai`. In that state the ChatGPT WHAM usage endpoint works with the stored token, but `openclaw status --usage --json` reports `usage.providers: []` / `Usage: no provider usage available`.

This preserves the new canonical `openai` usage provider while making usage auth backward-compatible with existing `openai-codex` OAuth/token profiles until users run doctor/migration.

## Real behavior proof
- Behavior or issue addressed: `openclaw status --usage` returned no provider usage when the only live ChatGPT OAuth profile was stored under the legacy `openai-codex:*` provider id.
- Real environment tested: Mac mini running installed OpenClaw 2026.6.1 with the real main-agent profile store; the profile id/provider was inspected with secrets redacted (`openai-codex:*`, OAuth, account id present).
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran the local fallback command against the same real profile after resolving the legacy `openai-codex` OAuth token to the canonical OpenAI usage endpoint: `/Users/cyberowl/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/codex-usage-status.sh`.
- Evidence after fix: Redacted terminal output from the real setup:

```text
Usage:
5h: 94% left · used 6% · reset 04.06.2026, 23:00
Week: 98% left · used 2% · reset 11.06.2026, 05:52
Plan: pro
Source: direct ChatGPT WHAM fallback
```

- Observed result after fix: The same real `openai-codex:*` OAuth profile produced live ChatGPT WHAM usage windows for 5h and Week instead of `usage.providers: []`. The branch makes the built-in usage auth resolver use that legacy profile for canonical `openai` usage, including the plugin path that explicitly asks for `resolveOAuthToken({ provider: "openai" })`.
- What was not tested: Not tested against a second real machine with a migrated SQLite `openai:*` profile; the existing behavior for canonical profiles is covered by current paths and the added regression tests focus on the legacy profile compatibility.

## Tests
- `CI=true pnpm vitest run src/infra/provider-usage.shared.test.ts src/infra/provider-usage.auth.normalizes-keys.test.ts`

target.title: fix(agent): align gateway CLI timeout default
target.body:
## Summary

- align `openclaw agent` / agent-via-gateway's omitted timeout with the shared 48h agent runtime default
- keep explicitly configured `agents.defaults.timeoutSeconds` and `--timeout` overrides unchanged
- remove generic 600s agent timeout examples from gateway docs so users do not copy an accidental 10-minute cap

## Issue / Motivation

Fixes #98180.

The embedded runtime defaults unset `agents.defaults.timeoutSeconds` to 48h, but agent-via-gateway still fell back to 600s when config omitted a timeout. That made CLI/gateway-dispatched agent runs shorter than the documented runtime default and reinforced docs examples that looked like a general default.

## Changes

- Export the shared `DEFAULT_AGENT_TIMEOUT_SECONDS` constant from `src/agents/timeout.ts`.
- Use that constant in `src/commands/agent-via-gateway.ts` when neither `--timeout` nor `agents.defaults.timeoutSeconds` is set.
- Add regression coverage for omitted timeout using `172_800` seconds and for explicit configured timeouts.
- Update gateway docs examples to omit `timeoutSeconds: 600` and call out short timeouts as intentional bounds only.

## Testing

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/agent-via-gateway.test.ts src/agents/subagent-depth.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- Secret scan over `git diff` added lines: `SECRET_SCAN_MATCHES 0`

target.body:
## Summary

Closes #72572.

This restores support for dotfile-managed `~/.openclaw` directories while keeping exec approval paths hardened on both the Node and macOS node-host paths.

- allow trusted first-level `~/.openclaw` symlinks on POSIX after validating link ownership, link location, target ownership, target permissions, and stable resolved ancestors
- keep Windows `.openclaw` symlink targets blocked until ACL validation exists
- keep rejecting user-controlled chained symlink targets, deeper symlink traversal, and symlinked `exec-approvals.json` destinations
- fail closed for unsafe approvals reads in runtime policy paths while keeping snapshot/control-plane reads usable
- apply equivalent state-dir, JSON store, and socket-parent hardening on macOS, including symlinked `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` overrides and system `/tmp`/`/var` symlink roots
- document the supported symlink shape in approvals CLI docs

## Validation

- `pnpm docs:list`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/infra/exec-approvals.ts src/infra/exec-approvals-store.test.ts`

target.title: feat(doctor): warn when cron delivery targets inactive channel
target.body:
Related: #97496 (generic channel-plugin activation blockers — complementary, does not inspect cron jobs)

## What Problem This Solves

Resolves a problem where a persisted cron job that pins a concrete `delivery.channel`
(for example `slack`) keeps that target after an update or plugin change deactivates the
channel plugin, then **silently fails to deliver at its next scheduled run** with no
warning ahead of time. `openclaw doctor` already inspects the cron store (legacy
migration, `payload.model` overrides) but never checks whether each job's concrete
delivery target still maps to an active channel plugin, so the drift is invisible until
the run fails.

## Why This Change Was Made

Adds an observer-only doctor advisory: when the cron store is inspected, each job whose
resolved primary delivery plan names a **concrete** announce channel is checked against
the active channel plugins for the current config, and a `Cron` note lists any whose
plugin is not active. It is detection only — it never repairs jobs, reactivates channels,
or writes config. Pseudo/relative targets are intentionally skipped because they resolve
at run time and cannot be validated ahead: announce-to-`last`, `webhook`, `none`, and
jobs with no explicit delivery object. The concrete-vs-pseudo decision reuses the existing
`resolveCronDeliveryPlan`, and availability reuses `listReadOnlyChannelPluginsForConfig`
with the same options the doctor channel checks use (`includeSetupFallbackPlugins: true`),
so configured channels are recognized even when no gateway is running and are never
mistaken for unavailable ones. Channel resolution is lazy (only when a job pins a concrete
channel) and best-effort (a resolution failure emits no advisory), so the common doctor
path pays no extra cost and the check never breaks the cron flow. The shared
`formatProviderCounts` helper is generalized to `formatSortedCounts` now that two
advisories use it.

## User Impact

Operators running `openclaw doctor` now get an early, actionable warning when a scheduled
cron job points at a channel whose plugin is no longer active — for example after removing
a channel or changing plugins — instead of discovering the silent delivery failure only
after the next run is missed. The note names the affected job(s) and channel(s) and points
to `openclaw cron list` / `openclaw cron show <job-id>` to reactivate the plugin or update
the target. Jobs that deliver to `last`, via webhook, or with no channel are unaffected, and
when every concrete target resolves to an active channel doctor prints nothing new.

## Evidence

Observer-only; no config schema, CLI flag, or persisted data-model change.

Focused tests (10 new collector tests + 30 existing doctor-cron tests, non-breaking wiring):

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run \
    src/commands/doctor/cron/warnings.test.ts \
    src/commands/doctor/cron/index.test.ts --reporter=dot
 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  40 passed (40)
```

Typecheck (core prod + core test lanes): 0 errors.

```
$ node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json ...            # 0 errors
$ node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json ...  # 0 errors
```

Real production-path run — the real `listReadOnlyChannelPluginsForConfig` resolver, the
real `resolveCronDeliveryPlan`, and the real `note` emit, driven against an isolated
`OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` with telegram configured and slack absent (redacted token / temp paths;
no mocks):

```
resolved available channel ids (real resolver): ["telegram"]

◇  Cron ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│  Cron delivery targets unavailable channels at <state>/cron/jobs.sqlite.│
│  - 1 job announces to a channel whose plugin is not active; the next    │
│    scheduled run will fail to deliver                                    │
│  - Channels: slack=1                                                     │
│  - Examples: weekly-report -> slack                                     │
│  Reactivate the channel plugin or update the job's `delivery.channel`   │
│  after reviewing with openclaw cron list and openclaw cron show <job-id>.│
╰─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
```

The same run leaves the configured `telegram` job, an announce-to-`last` job, and a webhook
job unflagged, confirming concrete-vs-pseudo classification on the real path.

target.title: fix(agent): allow silent-error retry for empty turns regardless of tool definitions
target.body:
## Summary

**Problem**: The `empty-error-retry` path in the embedded agent runner (`run.ts:3362-3383`) is gated on `shouldRetrySilentErrorAssistantTurn`, which checked `resolveAttemptReplayMetadata(params.attempt).hadPotentialSideEffects`. This flag reflects whether a transcript replay would be safe — but the silent-error retry is a model resubmission, not a transcript replay. The flag is derived from `buildAttemptReplayMetadata` which reads agent-level tool definitions (`toolMetas`), not actual tool calls executed in the current turn. Any agent with a non-`replaySafe` tool in its configuration (e.g. `exec`) permanently fails this check, even when the current attempt produced zero output, executed zero tool calls, and should be retried.

In production this causes silent terminal failures: a transient HTTP 5xx from the provider returns `stopReason="error"` with zero output tokens, the empty-error retry path is blocked by `hadPotentialSideEffects` from a prior turn's tool definition, and the user receives no reply and no diagnostic — the session goes idle silently.

**Solution**: Remove the `hadPotentialSideEffects` check from `shouldRetrySilentErrorAssistantTurn`. The function already guards on `hasAttemptTerminalState`, which catches concrete tool execution outcomes (actual `clientToolCalls`, `yieldDetected`, delivery, session spawns, cron writes) — the cases where a retry genuinely should be blocked. The `hadPotentialSideEffects` flag from static tool definitions only matters for transcript replays where tool results would be duplicated; it should not gate model resubmission of a failed turn that produced zero output.

**What changed**: `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/incomplete-turn.ts` — +10/-3. Removed the `replayMetadata` Pick and `resolveAttemptReplayMetadata` call from `shouldRetrySilentErrorAssistantTurn`. Updated the JSDoc to document the distinction between model resubmission and transcript replay. `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts` — +7/-2. Renamed test "does not retry errored thinking-only turns after side effects" → "retries errored thinking-only turns regardless of static replay metadata (#97877)" and updated expectation from `false` to `true`.

**What did NOT change**: `resolveAttemptReplayMetadata`, `buildAttemptReplayMetadata`, `hasAttemptTerminalState`, the `empty-error-retry` loop in `run.ts`, `resolveIncompleteTurnPayloadText` (which still uses `hadPotentialSideEffects` for its user-facing warning text — that is correct behavior), `shouldSkipNonVisibleTurnRetry`, `shouldRetryMissingAssistantTurn`, `resolveEmptyResponseRetryInstruction`, or any other retry path.

Fixes #97877

## What Problem This Solves

In production deployments where agents use tools like `exec` (bash), any prior tool call in a session permanently sets `hadPotentialSideEffects = true` for that session's accumulated replay state. When a subsequent turn gets a transient HTTP 5xx from the provider (e.g. OpenRouter returning 500 to `/chat/completions`), the `empty-error-retry` path — which has `MAX_EMPTY_ERROR_RETRIES = 3` — is blocked because `shouldRetrySilentErrorAssistantTurn` reads `resolveAttemptReplayMetadata(attempt).hadPotentialSideEffects` which is true from the agent's tool definitions, not from any actual tool execution in the current turn.

The user experiences this as a silent session death: no reply, no diagnostic, no retry. The session file ends with `stopReason="error"` and zero output tokens. The `[empty-error-retry]` log line that should appear (`stopReason=error non-visible-output; resubmitting attempt=N/3`) is never emitted because the retry guard short-circuits.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: Silent terminal failure when a transient provider HTTP 5xx occurs in a session that previously executed a tool call — the `empty-error-retry` path is blocked by `hadPotentialSideEffects` from static agent tool definitions, even though the current turn produced zero output and zero tool calls.

**Real setup tested**:
- **Runtime**: Node 24.13, Linux x86_64
- **Branch**: `fix/issue-97877-empty-error-retry` at `c679c1c5d5`
- **Test runner**: `scripts/run-vitest.mjs`

**Root cause**: `shouldRetrySilentErrorAssistantTurn` checked `resolveAttemptReplayMetadata(params.attempt).hadPotentialSideEffects`. `hadPotentialSideEffects` is derived from agent-level tool definitions (`toolMetas`) via `buildAttemptReplayMetadata`: `hadUnsafeTools = toolMetas.some(entry => entry.replaySafe !== true)`. Any agent with a non-replaySafe tool (e.g. `exec`, which is the most common agent tool) has this flag permanently true. The silent-error retry is a model resubmission — no tool results are re-executed — so the static tool-definition flag is the wrong gate.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts`

**After-fix evidence**:
```
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.src.config.ts
 RUN  v4.1.8

 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  222 passed (222)
   Start at  22:04:43
   Duration  101.43s

[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in ~101s
```

Updated test: "retries errored thinking-only turns regardless of static replay metadata (#97877)". A turn with `replayMetadata.hadPotentialSideEffects = true` but only redacted thinking content is now correctly retry-eligible — the model resubmission gate is `hasAttemptTerminalState`, not static tool definitions.

**Observed result after the fix**: The `empty-error-retry` path in `run.ts` is no longer blocked by `hadPotentialSideEffects` in `shouldRetrySilentErrorAssistantTurn`. The existing guards — `hasAttemptTerminalState`, `stopReason === "error"`, zero visible text, `MAX_EMPTY_ERROR_RETRIES = 3` — continue to protect against inappropriate retries. The fix aligns the gate with the operation: a model resubmission, not a transcript replay.

**What was not tested**: Live transient HTTP 5xx from OpenRouter with an agent that has `exec` tool configured. The fix is at the retry eligibility layer — it removes one precondition from a boolean function that was blocking the retry. The remaining guards (`hasAttemptTerminalState`, stopReason check, auth/rate-limit checks) are sufficient to prevent inappropriate retries. The existing 222 tests in the incomplete-turn test file pass unchanged (with the updated test now reflecting correct behavior).

**Evidence provenance**: `scripts/run-vitest.mjs` on the PR head at Node 24.13 / Linux x86_64, 2026-06-30.

## Evidence

### Vitest (222/222)

All tests pass, including:
- "retries replay-safe errored turns that only emitted thinking blocks" ✅
- "does not retry errored empty turns when non-zero output may indicate progress" ✅
- "does not retry errored turns containing visible text / tool call / unknown block" ✅
- "retries errored thinking-only turns regardless of static replay metadata (#97877)" ✅ (updated)
- Empty response retry tests (OpenAI, Bedrock, Gemini, GPT patterns) ✅

Zero regressions. The fix is a one-gate removal.

## Tests and validation

| Test Type | Result | Details |
|-----------|--------|---------|
| All existing incomplete-turn tests | ✅ 221/221 | Zero regressions |
| Updated side-effects regression test | ✅ 1/1 | Now correctly returns true (#97877) |
| Total | ✅ 222/222 | |

## Code walkthrough

The fix is in `shouldRetrySilentErrorAssistantTurn` at `incomplete-turn.ts:524`.

**Before:**
```typescript
export function shouldRetrySilentErrorAssistantTurn(params: {
  attempt: Pick<EmbeddedRunAttemptResult, ... | "replayMetadata">;
  ...
}): boolean {
  if (joinAssistantTexts(params.attempt.assistantTexts).length > 0) return false;
  if (hasAttemptTerminalState(params.attempt)) return false;
  if (resolveAttemptReplayMetadata(params.attempt).hadPotentialSideEffects) return false; // ← REMOVED
  ...
}
```

**After:**
```typescript
export function shouldRetrySilentErrorAssistantTurn(params: {
  attempt: Pick<EmbeddedRunAttemptResult, ...>; // replayMetadata removed from Pick
  ...
}): boolean {
  if (joinAssistantTexts(params.attempt.assistantTexts).length > 0) return false;
  if (hasAttemptTerminalState(params.attempt)) return false;
  // hadPotentialSideEffects check removed — see JSDoc
  ...
}
```

The function is called at `run.ts:3372`:
```typescript
silentErrorRetryReason &&
shouldRetrySilentErrorAssistantTurn({ attempt, assistant: assistantForFailover }) &&
emptyErrorRetries < MAX_EMPTY_ERROR_RETRIES
```

The `silentErrorRetryReason` gate already filters to null/unclassified/unknown failover reasons. The `MAX_EMPTY_ERROR_RETRIES = 3` guard limits blast radius. The per-attempt `hasAttemptTerminalState` check still catches concrete tool-sourced terminal states.

## Design decisions

**Why remove hadPotentialSideEffects instead of scoping it to current-turn tools only?** The `hadPotentialSideEffects` flag is designed for transcript replay safety — "can we safely replay this attempt's tool results?" The silent-error retry is a fundamentally different operation: "can we resubmit this prompt to the model?" No tool results are re-executed, so replay safety is irrelevant. The right gate for model resubmission is `hasAttemptTerminalState` — did the current attempt actually execute tools, deliver messages, or spawn sessions? If not, resubmission is safe.

**Why keep hadPotentialSideEffects in resolveIncompleteTurnPayloadText?** The user-facing warning text correctly distinguishes between "no side effects, try again" and "tools may have run, verify before retrying." This provides valuable information to the user about whether they can safely re-trigger the prompt.

## Risk checklist

**What is the highest-risk area of this change?** A turn that DID execute tools but whose tool results are somehow invisible to `hasAttemptTerminalState` could be incorrectly retried. Mitigated by the extensive existing guards in `hasAttemptTerminalState` which covers `clientToolCalls`, `yieldDetected`, `didDeliverSourceReplyViaMessageTool`, `successfulCronAdds`, `acceptedSessionSpawns`, `hasCommittedMessagingToolDeliveryEvidence`, `hasAsyncStartedToolActivity`, and `lastToolError`.

**Risk level**: Low — the fix removes one static-definition-based gate from a retry eligibility function, replacing it with reliance on `hasAttemptTerminalState` which already catches actual tool execution outcomes. The `MAX_EMPTY_ERROR_RETRIES = 3` cap limits any potential retry loop to 3 iterations. The `silentErrorRetryReason` gate already filters to non-auth/non-rate-limit/non-billing failures.

**Merge risk declaration**: No merge risk. Pure removal of one boolean gate in a helper function. No config, protocol, API, or SDK surface changes. The call site in `run.ts` passes the full attempt object which still contains `replayMetadata` — the function simply no longer reads it.

## Linked context

| Issue/PR | Status | Relationship |
|----------|--------|-------------|
| #97877 | Open | Active canonical tracker — this PR targets it |
| #91953 | Closed (fixed) | Adjacent — thinking/nonzero-output empty-error retry guards, not the side-effect replay metadata blocker |
| #94176 | Open | Adjacent — missing diagnostics for non-deliverable terminal turns |
| #97324 | Open | Adjacent — broader replay-unsafe incomplete-turn state handling |

## How the empty-error-retry path works

The retry loop in `run.ts` processes each model-call attempt. After the assistant failover classification determines the failure reason, it evaluates five retry paths in priority order:

1. **Reasoning-only retry** — thinking blocks but no visible text → visible-answer continuation
2. **Missing-assistant retry** — no assistant turn at all → same-prompt resubmit (1 retry max)
3. **Empty-response retry** — zero payload, non-error stopReason → visible-answer continuation
4. **Empty-error retry** ← **THIS IS THE FIXED PATH** — stopReason="error", zero visible output → same-prompt resubmit (3 retries max, `MAX_EMPTY_ERROR_RETRIES = 3`)
5. **Compaction continuation retry** — compaction interrupted visible answer → retry with compacted transcript

Path 4 was the only retry path gated on `hadPotentialSideEffects`. Paths 1-2 use `shouldSkipNonVisibleTurnRetry` (which also checks `hadPotentialSideEffects`) but those paths involve visible-answer continuation instructions, not pure model resubmission. The fix removes the gate from Path 4 only — the other paths' `hadPotentialSideEffects` checks remain unchanged because they involve different retry semantics.

## Current review state

This is a focused, minimal fix: one boolean gate removed from one helper function. All 222 existing tests pass. The Renamed regression test confirms the correct behavior. No retry loop parameters, rate limits, or failover classifications were changed.

This is a focused fix for a narrow gate-removal in the empty-error retry path. All 222 existing tests pass. The updated test confirms that `hadPotentialSideEffects` from static agent tool definitions no longer blocks the empty-error retry for a turn that produced zero output and zero tool calls.

## Test detail for the updated regression

The renamed test constructs a turn with `replayMetadata.hadPotentialSideEffects = true` (the exact scenario reported in #97877 — a prior tool call left this flag set) and only redacted thinking content (zero visible output, `usage.output = 1120 > 0`). Before the fix, `shouldRetrySilentErrorAssistantTurn` returned `false` because `resolveAttemptReplayMetadata(attempt).hadPotentialSideEffects` was `true`. After the fix, it returns `true` because `hasAttemptTerminalState` (which checks actual tool execution) finds no concrete terminal state — only `hasOnlyAssistantReasoningContent` matters for eligibility.

This test directly exercises the production code path at `run.ts:3372` — the same function, the same conditions, the same failure mode as the reporter's OpenRouter 500 scenario.

## Architecture context

The embedded agent runner maintains two layers of replay/side-effect tracking:

| Layer | Data structure | Scope | Used by |
|-------|---------------|-------|---------|
| Per-attempt | `EmbeddedRunAttemptResult.replayMetadata` | Current attempt only | `shouldRetrySilentErrorAssistantTurn` (before fix), `resolveIncompleteTurnPayloadText` |
| Accumulated | `accumulatedReplayState` (in `run.ts`) | All attempts in the run | `incompleteTurnFallbackSafe` (line 3863), compaction retry guard (line 3879) |

The per-attempt `replayMetadata` is built by `buildAttemptReplayMetadata` which checks `toolMetas` (the agent tool definitions loaded for this attempt), not actual tool execution outcomes. The accumulated state is built by `observeReplayMetadata` which merges per-attempt metadata across the entire run.

The empty-error retry uses the per-attempt `replayMetadata` (via `resolveAttemptReplayMetadata`). The fix removes this check because the per-attempt metadata reflects static tool definitions — not whether the current turn actually executed any tools.

### Concrete scenario (reproducible from source)

1. Agent has `exec` tool in its configuration (`replaySafe !== true`)
2. Turn N: Agent calls `exec gh pr list`, gets tool result → `buildAttemptReplayMetadata` records `hadPotentialSideEffects: true` for Turn N
3. `accumulatedReplayState` merges Turn N's metadata → `accumulatedReplayState.hadPotentialSideEffects = true`
4. Turn N+1: User sends follow-up prompt, model call immediately returns HTTP 500
5. `buildAttemptReplayMetadata` for Turn N+1: still `hadPotentialSideEffects: true` because Turn N+1 still has `exec` in its `toolMetas` (even though no tool was called in Turn N+1)
6. `shouldRetrySilentErrorAssistantTurn` at run.ts:3372 returns `false` → retry skipped → session goes idle

After the fix, step 6 proceeds to `MAX_EMPTY_ERROR_RETRIES = 3` retries because `hasAttemptTerminalState(Turn N+1)` returns `false` (no tool was actually called in Turn N+1).


The fix is source-provable: removing a single return-false guard from  cannot introduce regressions in the 5 other retry paths, each of which has independent eligibility guards.

target.title: fix: Tailscale serve creates redundant entries on gateway startup
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where `gateway.tailscale.mode: "serve"` could accumulate duplicate Tailscale Serve handlers across gateway restarts because `tailscale serve --bg --yes <port>` re-applies Serve state instead of cleaning stale entries first.

Fixes #93936

## Why This Change Was Made

Startup cleanup now removes only the OpenClaw-owned Serve route before re-enabling it. The default node route uses `tailscale serve --https=443 off`, while configured service routes use `tailscale serve clear <serviceName>`, avoiding the previous broad `tailscale serve reset` behavior that could delete unrelated operator-managed Serve routes.

The unrelated `.codegraph/.gitignore` file was removed from this branch.

## User Impact

Gateway restarts can refresh OpenClaw's Tailscale Serve route without wiping other Serve configuration on the same node.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: Tailscale Serve startup cleanup is scoped to OpenClaw's route instead of resetting all Serve config.

**Real environment tested**: macOS local checkout, Node 24.13.1.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/tailscale.test.ts src/gateway/server-tailscale.test.ts
node --import tsx /Users/yangjiajun/projects/auto-pr/workspace/openclaw/outputs/issue-93936-pr-94496/verify-scoped-serve-cleanup.mjs
```

**Evidence after fix**:

```text
[test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 9.43s

Tests       55 passed (55)
```

The scoped-cleanup proof runs the real `enableTailscaleServe()` helper against a local fake Tailscale CLI and records the exact CLI arguments:

```json
{
  "commands": [
    "serve --https=443 off",
    "serve --bg --yes 3000",
    "serve clear svc:openclaw",
    "serve --service=svc:openclaw --bg --yes 3000"
  ],
  "globalResetUsed": false
}
```

**Observed result after fix**: The default no-service path no longer calls `tailscale serve reset`; it clears only the default HTTPS Serve route before re-adding OpenClaw's route. The service-name path remains scoped to the configured service.

**What was not tested**: A live Tailscale tailnet restart loop with `tailscale serve status --json`. The command contract was checked against Tailscale upstream source: `serve reset` clears the full Serve config, `serve clear <service>` clears one service, and `serve --https=443 off` unsets the default node HTTPS route.

AI-assisted: built with Codex

target.title: fix(restart): add error logging to fire-and-forget restart emissions
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

`emitPreparedGatewayRestart()` is async but called with `void` from synchronous functions. Previously, any error during restart emission was silently lost — the gateway might fail to restart without any diagnostic.

## Changes

Moved the failure diagnostic from 7 caller-side `.catch()` blocks into `emitPreparedGatewayRestart()` itself, where `emitGatewayRestart()` returns false. This centralizes the warning in one place.

## Evidence

**Unit tests (60/60 passed):**
```
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  60 passed (60)
```

**Real behavior proof — second call returns false (already pending signal):**
```
$ ./node_modules/.bin/tsx _restart_proof.mjs

=== Real behavior proof — restart emission failure warning ===

1. First emitGatewayRestart(): true  (signal emitted)
2. Second emitGatewayRestart(): false (already pending)
   → restartLog.warn() fires here
3. After consume: reset state
```

**Log output (after fix):**
- On first call: restart signal emitted successfully
- On second call (pending signal): `restart emission failed: another signal pending or emission rolled back` is logged via `restartLog.warn()`

## Review
- Manually reviewed and verified
- AI-assisted: Yes

target.title: [AI] fix(agents): recover from non-resumable assistant tail by re-presenting last user message
target.body:
## Summary

Gateway restart drops text-only assistant-tail sessions, forcing user retype. Add resume-from-transcript logic to recover text-only tails by re-presenting the last user message via `buildResumeMessage()` with idempotency guard.
- Problem: Text-only assistant tails after gateway restart forced user retype
- Solution: Extract last user message from transcript and re-present via `buildResumeMessage()` with idempotency guard
- What changed: `src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.ts` (resume path + tests)
- What did NOT change: Tool-call and approval-pending tails remain fail-closed (#95609)

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Fixes #95899
- Related #95609 (broader no-retype policy)
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

After a gateway restart, sessions with a text-only assistant tail (the agent replied with text, no pending tool calls or approval) were silently dropped — the user had to retype their request. The existing recovery code only handled raw API message formats and the physical last message, missing text-only canonical content-block representations and trailing system/compaction messages. This PR closes that gap so text-only tails are transparently recovered.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: Gateway restart recovery of text-only assistant-tail sessions — auto-resume without user retype
- Real environment tested: Linux 4.19.112, Node 22, branch `fix/restart-recovery-95899`
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

  ```text
  # E2E proof: real gateway process + production recovery functions
  npx tsx proof-95899.mjs

  # Unit test matrix: assistant tail variants
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.test.ts --run
  ```

- Evidence after fix:

  E2E live gateway output:

  ```text
  Gateway: starting...                    ✓
  Mark:     marked=1 abortedLastRun=true  ✓
  Recover:  recovered=1 failed=0 skipped=0  ✓
  Session:  status=running abortedLastRun=false  ✓

  ✅ GATEWAY RESTART RECOVERY: recovered=1 failed=0
     Text-only assistant tail → auto-resumed
  ```

  Recovery tail-type matrix (unit test):

  | Tail type | Result | User experience |
  |-----------|--------|-----------------|
  | Text-only assistant | `recovered=1` | Agent sees original request, continues |
  | tool_use (snake_case) | `failed=1` | User sees resend notice |
  | toolcall (lowercase) | `failed=1` | User sees resend notice |
  | tooluse (lowercase) | `failed=1` | User sees resend notice |
  | functioncall (lowercase) | `failed=1` | User sees resend notice |
  | tool_call (content block) | `skipped=1` | User sees resend notice |
  | Approval-pending | `failed=1` | User sees resend notice |
  | Empty/uninspectable | `skipped=1` | User sees resend notice |

  Unit test output:

  ```text
  $ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.test.ts --run
  ✓ recovers from non-resumable assistant tail by re-presenting last user message
  ✓ falls back to unresumable notice when assistant tail has pending tool_calls
  ✓ falls back to unresumable notice when assistant tail has content-block tool_calls
  ✓ gates tool_use (snake_case) assistant tails from auto-resume
  ✓ gates toolcall (lowercase) assistant tails from auto-resume
  ✓ gates tooluse (lowercase) assistant tails from auto-resume
  ✓ gates functioncall (lowercase) assistant tails from auto-resume
  ✓ gates tool-call recovery when system message trails assistant with tool_calls
  ✓ still fails on stale approval-pending tool result tail
  ✓ omits idempotency guard when assistant tail has no pending tool_calls
  ```

- Observed result after fix:
  1. Text-only assistant tail sessions auto-resume with `recovered=1`
  2. `abortedLastRun` cleared after recovery — session status returns to `running`
  3. All tool-call variants (6 formats including lowercase aliases) stay fail-closed
  4. Approval-pending tails still correctly fail-closed
  5. Unit tests pass — 52 tests across all recovery paths
- What was not tested: Browser UI flow, multi-turn sessions with compaction artifacts (transcript parsing is covered by unit tests), cross-channel delivery after recovery
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: The e2e proof used loopback networking with `--auth none` — no token auth or TLS tested. The session transcript was hand-crafted (not a real interrupted session). Unit tests cover the production JSONL parsing path.

## Root Cause (if applicable) ✅ optional

N/A — functional gap, not a root-cause bug fix.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable) ✅ optional

N/A — new behavior, no existing tests to regress.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Text-only assistant-tail sessions after gateway restart are now transparently recovered — the user does not need to retype. Tool-call and approval-pending tails still show a resend notice (unchanged).

## Diagram (if applicable) ✅ optional

```text
Recovery flow (text-only tail):

  markRestartAbortedMainSessions()
    → sets abortedLastRun=true
  recoverRestartAbortedMainSessions()
    → readSessionMessagesAsync()
    → parsedSessionEntryToMessage() ← requires {"message": {…}} format
    → findLastUserMessage()          ← finds last user message in transcript
    → buildResumeMessage()           ← wraps in <untrusted-text>
    → callGateway({ method: "agent" }) ← resumes via WS
    → clears abortedLastRun
```

## Security Impact (required)

- [x] New permissions/capabilities? No
- [x] Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- [x] New/changed network calls? No
- [x] Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- [x] Data access scope changed? No

## Reproductive + Verification ✅ optional

### Environment

- OS: Linux 4.19.112
- Runtime: Node 22
- Branch: `fix/restart-recovery-95899`

### Steps

1. Start gateway with text-only config
2. Create session with text-only assistant tail
3. Call `markRestartAbortedMainSessions({ stateDir, sessionKeys: [KEY] })`
4. Call `recoverRestartAbortedMainSessions({ stateDir })`
5. Verify `recovered=1, failed=0`

### Expected

recovered=1, abortedLastRun cleared, session status=running

### Actual

```
Recover:  recovered=1 failed=0 skipped=0
Session:  status=running abortedLastRun=false
```

## Evidence ✅ optional

- [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Key findings from e2e proof:
1. **Transcript format** — JSONL entries must use `{"message": {"role": "user", ...}}` wrapper, not bare `{"role": "user"}`
2. **Config isolation** — `callGateway`'s config loader diverges when `OPENCLAWS_CONFIG` is set in caller env; recovery process must only set `OPENCLAWS_STATE_DIR`
3. **External recovery** — `recoverRestartAbortedMainSessions` called from outside the gateway process successfully connects via WS to the running gateway

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: text-only assistant tail recovery (both unit test and real gateway)
- Edge cases checked: tool-call tails (6 formats), approval-pending tails, empty/uninspectable tails
- What you did NOT verify: browser UI, mobile app flow, multi-channel delivery after recovery

## Review Conversations ✅ optional

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- [x] Backward compatible? Yes
- [ ] Config/env changes? No
- [ ] Migration needed? No

## Best-fix Verdict (from Review Contract)

Is this change the best way to solve the problem?

- **Best fix**: Yes — the recovery path already existed for API-level transcripts; this extends it to text-only content-block format which is the common case
- **Refactor needed**: No
- **Alternative considered**: Full session replay from transcript (too invasive, #95609 tracks broader policy)

## AI Assistance 🤖 (required for AI-assisted PRs)

- **AI-assisted**: Yes
- **AI model**: deepseek-v4-pro
- **Human confirmed understanding of code changes**: Yes
- **AI prompts / session excerpts**: Investigation focused on why `callGateway({ method: "agent" })` fails when called from within gateway process; root cause was transcript JSONL format mismatch and config env var leakage

## Risks and Mitigations

**Highest-risk area**: Compatibility for sessions with non-standard transcript formats (compaction artifacts, system messages appended after assistant tail). **Mitigation**: If transcript parsing fails to find a user message, recovery falls through to existing fail-closed behavior (skipped=1, user sees resend notice) — no data loss or phantom state.

No other risks.

---

Fixes #95899

target.title: fix(media): allow private openai compatible audio transcription endpoints
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Audio media-understanding transcription could target self-hosted OpenAI-compatible endpoints, but audio config did not expose the private-network request opt-in used by guarded provider HTTP.
- Why it matters: Trusted local, RFC1918, or tailnet Whisper-style endpoints need explicit private-network access without globally relaxing media request SSRF policy.
- What changed: Added audio-only request config support for `tools.media.audio.request.allowPrivateNetwork`, aligned schema/generated metadata/docs, threaded audio request overrides into provider execution, and aligned OpenAI-compatible loopback audio auth with the chat-completion local auth pattern by using the shared `custom-local` marker only for loopback OpenAI-compatible audio bases.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): Image and video media request config still reject `allowPrivateNetwork`, private-network access remains disabled by default, explicit `request.auth` still injects the configured auth header, and media/audio does not add the Ollama-specific local-auth branch.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [x] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes N/A
- Related N/A
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Audio media config reused the generic media request schema/type path, which rejected `allowPrivateNetwork`, while provider execution needed an audio-specific trusted request override path. The local no-auth path also needed to follow the existing chat-completion pattern: synthesize the shared `custom-local` marker for loopback OpenAI-compatible endpoints, then avoid sending provider-default auth for that marker.
- Missing detection / guardrail: Existing schema and runner tests did not cover audio-specific private-network request overrides or loopback OpenAI-compatible audio endpoints without provider auth.
- Contributing context (if known): The config docs baseline and audio field metadata are generated/aligned surfaces for this config contract.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/config/schema.test.ts`, `src/media-understanding/openai-compatible-audio.test.ts`, `src/media-understanding/runner.proxy.test.ts`, `src/media-understanding/shared.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: Audio config accepts and forwards `allowPrivateNetwork`, image/video reject it, per-model audio request config works, loopback OpenAI-compatible audio can synthesize `custom-local` when provider auth is absent, synthetic local audio auth sends no provider-default `Authorization`, explicit request auth still wins, and Ollama does not get the media/audio local-auth branch.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: These tests exercise the config contract and provider request assembly without needing a live transcription service.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): N/A
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Users can opt audio transcription requests into private-network endpoints with `tools.media.audio.request.allowPrivateNetwork` and per-model audio request config. Loopback OpenAI-compatible audio endpoints such as `http://127.0.0.1:8000/v1` can run without provider auth, matching local OpenAI-compatible chat-completion providers. The OpenAI provider docs now show the loopback example base URL.

## Diagram (if applicable)

N/A

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: The new network capability is an explicit audio-only opt-in for trusted private endpoints; defaults remain blocked by the SSRF guard and image/video still reject the private-network flag. Local no-auth audio is restricted to loopback OpenAI-compatible audio providers using the existing non-secret `custom-local` marker. Explicit request auth still injects the configured header when present.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux 6.1.0-44-cloud-amd64 x86_64
- Runtime/container: Node v22.22.2, pnpm 10.33.0
- Model/provider: OpenAI-compatible audio transcription config path
- Integration/channel (if any): Media understanding audio attachments
- Relevant config (redacted): `tools.media.audio.request.allowPrivateNetwork`, `tools.media.audio.request.auth`, `tools.media.audio.models[].baseUrl`

### Steps

1. Parse audio, image, and video media config shapes through the runtime zod schema.
2. Run audio provider execution with provider-level, audio-level, and per-model request overrides.
3. Run provider HTTP request assembly with synthetic local auth and explicit request auth.

### Expected

- Audio request config accepts and forwards private-network opt-in only when explicitly configured.
- Image/video reject `allowPrivateNetwork` under media request config.
- Loopback OpenAI-compatible audio can use the shared synthetic local auth marker when provider auth is absent.
- Synthetic local auth does not become a provider-default `Authorization` header.
- Explicit request auth overrides synthetic local auth and injects the configured header.
- Ollama does not receive a media/audio local-auth special case.

### Actual

- Matches expected in local tests and changed-gate validation.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Local validation:

- `pnpm docs:list`
- `pnpm test src/media-understanding/openai-compatible-audio.test.ts src/media-understanding/runner.proxy.test.ts src/media-understanding/shared.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/model-auth.ts src/media-understanding/runner.entries.ts src/media-understanding/runner.proxy.test.ts src/media-understanding/runner.test-mocks.ts docs/providers/openai.md docs/nodes/audio.md CHANGELOG.md src/media-understanding/openai-compatible-audio.ts src/media-understanding/openai-compatible-audio.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm check:changed`

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

Set up a (WhisperLive)[https://github.com/collabora/WhisperLive] server locally. Modified openclaw.json to point to it with `media.audio.request.allowPrivateNetwork = true`:

```
"media": {
      "audio": {
        "enabled": true,
        "language": "en",
        "models": [
          {
            "provider": "openai",
            "model": "whisper-1",
            "baseUrl": "http://127.0.0.1:8000/v1",
          }
        ],
        "request": {
          "allowPrivateNetwork": true,
        }
      }
```

Verified audio transcription:

```
$ openclaw capability audio transcribe --file tmp/media-test/transcription-test.wav --language en --json
{
  "ok": true,
  "capability": "audio.transcribe",
  "transport": "local",
  "attempts": [],
  "outputs": [
    {
      "path": "/home/openclaw/.openclaw/workspace/tmp/media-test/transcription-test.wav",
      "text": "Open Claw Media Transcription Test. The passphrase is Brass Lobster 7.",
      "kind": "audio.transcription"
    }
  ]
}
```



## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

No PR review conversations currently exist.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) Yes, optional new audio request config only
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Users could point audio transcription at private endpoints they do not fully trust.
  - Mitigation: The private-network exception is explicit, audio-only, disabled by default, documented as trusted-endpoint-only, and covered by schema/runtime tests.
- Risk: Synthetic local auth could accidentally be sent as provider-default auth.
  - Mitigation: OpenAI-compatible audio now uses the shared chat-completion local auth marker and omits provider-default auth for that marker while preserving explicit request auth overrides.

target.title: fix(agents): normalize bare date filenames to memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md in…
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes #92372.

Multi-agent secondary agents with explicit workspaces fail to read daily memory files when the model drops the `memory/` prefix. The startup context prelude loads daily memory as `memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md`, guidance tells the model it's "already included," and the model forms a bare `YYYY-MM-DD.md` path — hitting ENOENT at the workspace root.

Additionally, the ENOENT retry branch synthesized `memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md` and called `params.base.execute` directly, completely bypassing the outer `wrapToolWorkspaceRootGuardWithOptions` sandbox validation. A symlinked `memory/` directory pointing outside the workspace would escape the sandbox (**P1 security boundary**).

## Why This Change Was Made

- Relax `DAILY_MEMORY_PATH_RE` to also match bare date filenames, then prepend `memory/` in `normalizeDailyMemoryReadPath`.
- Flatten `executeReadPage` into a two-phase flow: Phase 1 tries the original path; Phase 2 retries with the canonical `memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md` path only on ENOENT.
- Validate the synthesized retry path through `assertSandboxPath` (the same async guard used by the outer wrapper, including real-path symlink/hardlink/alias resolution via `assertNoPathAliasEscape`) **before** any filesystem access.
- Pass the workspace root via an optional `workspaceRoot` string — no callback indirection. Only injected when `workspaceOnly` is true.
- Preserve existing behavior: root-level date files succeed on first attempt, `memory/`-prefixed paths are unchanged, non-date filenames are unaffected, and `missingDailyMemoryReadResult` soft-fail is preserved.

## User Impact

Secondary agents with explicit workspaces can now successfully read daily memory files even when the model sends a bare date filename. No stale ENOENT errors, no wasted tokens. All synthesized fallback paths remain strictly confined inside the workspace sandbox — a symlinked `memory/` directory cannot escape.

## Evidence

Base: `origin/main` at `081f8fd1fa`. Commits on `fix/read-tool-bare-date-filename-memory-prefix`.

### Full Test Suite (7 files, 124 tests, all passing)

```
$ pnpm test src/agents/agent-tools.workspace-paths.test.ts \
         src/agents/agent-tools.read.workspace-root-guard.test.ts \
         src/agents/agent-tools.create-openclaw-coding-tools.test.ts \
         src/agents/agent-tools.workspace-only-false.test.ts \
         src/agents/agent-tools.read.host-edit-access.test.ts \
         src/agents/agent-tools.read.arg-value-suffix.test.ts \
         src/agents/agent-tools.read.host-tilde-expansion.test.ts

 Test Files  7 passed (7)
      Tests  124 passed (124)
   Duration  44.39s
```

### End-to-End Reproduction: Secondary Agent + Isolated Workspace (#92372 Exact Chain)

Reproduces the exact multi-agent secondary sub-agent scenario from #92372 using `createOpenClawCodingTools` with `workspaceOnly: true` and an isolated workspace.

The chain matches #92372 verbatim:
1. Secondary sub-agent assigned independent isolated workspace
2. Context injects `memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md` (via `bootstrap-context`, memory-core plugin)
3. LLM autonomously emits bare `YYYY-MM-DD.md` (drops `memory/` prefix)
4. Phase 1: `base.execute` → ENOENT at workspace root
5. Phase 2: `normalizeDailyMemoryReadPath` → `memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md` → `assertSandboxPath` → `base.execute` → success

```
================================================================
  POST-FIX BEHAVIOR
================================================================

agentRole: secondary
workspaceIsolation: enabled
dailyMemoryFile: memory/2026-06-30.md  (exists in workspace)
contextInjected: "daily memory memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md loaded"
modelRequestedPath: 2026-06-30.md  (LLM drops memory/ prefix)

--- Post-fix regex validation ---
DAILY_MEMORY_PATH_RE: /^(?:memory\/)?\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}\.md$/
normalizeDailyMemoryReadPath("2026-06-30.md") → "memory/2026-06-30.md"

--- Full tool execution trace ---
phase1: ENOENT bare-date file
phase2: normalized path memory/2026-06-30.md
assertSandboxPath passed
read succeeded  (46ms)

toolResult_content:
  # 2026-06-30 User Preferences
  - Language: TypeScript, Rust
  - Editor: VS Code
  - Timezone: Asia/Shanghai

verdict: PASS — bare date filename auto-redirected.


================================================================
  PRE-FIX BASELINE (main branch — same scenario)
================================================================

agentRole: secondary
workspaceIsolation: enabled
dailyMemoryFile: memory/2026-06-30.md  (exists in workspace)
modelRequestedPath: 2026-06-30.md  (bare — memory/ prefix dropped)

DAILY_MEMORY_PATH_RE: /^memory\/\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}\.md$/
normalizeDailyMemoryReadPath("2026-06-30.md") → undefined

phase1: ENOENT
phase2: NOT TRIGGERED (regex requires "memory/" prefix)
verdict: FAIL — bare date filename returned ENOENT.


================================================================
  SYMLINK ESCAPE BLOCK
================================================================

agentRole: secondary
workspaceIsolation: enabled
memoryIsSymlink: true  (target outside workspace)

phase1: ENOENT bare-date file
phase2: normalized path memory/2026-06-30.md
assertSandboxPath: FAILED — Symlink escapes sandbox root
read: BLOCKED (no filesystem access to external directory)
verdict: PASS — symlink escape blocked before any file read.
```

### Real LLM Session: Gateway + Trajectory Heartbeat (Production Trace)

A full production agent was launched via `openclaw agent --local`. The ZTE/Qwen3-235B-A22B model **autonomously** emitted `read("2026-06-30.md")` after receiving the memory context injection — fully reproducing #92372 in a live agent session.

| Evidence | Source |
|---|---|
| `toolMetas: [{"toolName":"read","meta":"from 2026-06-30.md"}]` | Trajectory `seq 6` `trace.artifacts` |
| `assistantTexts: "# 2026-06-30 User Preferences..."` | Trajectory `seq 5` `model.completed` |
| `bootstrap-context: 24ms` (memory-core injection) | Gateway log `prep stages` |
| `finalStatus: success` | Trajectory `seq 6` `trace.artifacts` |

Full three-layer production log in [SESSION_EVIDENCE.md](SESSION_EVIDENCE.md).

### Kernel-Level Proof: `/proc/self/io` + `/proc/self/fd` Dual Audit

Directly reads Linux kernel `/proc/self/io` (maintained by `fs/read_write.c`, user-space cannot forge) and `/proc/self/fd` file descriptor table, proving **assertSandboxPath intercepts before the first read syscall**.

```json
{
  "test": "Kernel-level I/O audit",
  "hostname": "LIN-DF11F9C3F26.zte.intra",
  "platform": "linux",
  "kernel": "4.19.112-2.el8.x86_64",
  "result": { "blocked": true },
  "kernelIoAccounting": {
    "read_bytes": { "before": 171020288, "after": 171020288, "delta": 0 },
    "syscr": {
      "delta": 351,
      "note": "delta>0 is normal: /proc reads, epoll, socket, etc. (non-storage)"
    }
  },
  "fdAudit": {
    "openFdsPointingOutside": 0,
    "interpretation": "0 of 26 open fds point outside the workspace"
  },
  "verdict": "PASS: read_bytes delta=0, fd outside=0. Kernel never read the external file."
}
```

### Windows junction/reparse-point Equivalence (fs-safe source analysis)

`@openclaw/fs-safe` handles Windows junctions **identically** to POSIX symlinks:

| Mechanism        | POSIX              | Windows                             | fs-safe implementation                     |
| ---------------- | ------------------ | ----------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------ |
| Segment walk     | `path.sep`         | `path.win32.sep`                    | `root-path.js:84` auto-adapts              |
| stat check       | `isSymbolicLink()` | `isSymbolicLink()` (incl. junction) | Node.js reports junctions as symlinks      |
| realpath resolve | `fs.realpath`      | `fs.realpath` (native NTFS)         | `root-path.js:573` `resolveSymlinkHopPath` |
| Escape detection | `isPathInside`     | `isPathInside` + case normalization | `path.js:36-41` Windows branch             |
| Path comparison  | case-sensitive     | `normalizeWindowsPathForComparison` | `path.js:8-17` strips `\\?\` prefix        |

**Key code path**: `assertSandboxPath` → `assertNoPathAliasEscape` → `resolveRootPath` → `resolveSymlinkHopPath` → `fsp.realpath` (Windows natively resolves junction target) → `isPathInside` (checks canonical path against root) → `symlinkEscapeError`. **Windows CI limitation**: junction creation requires administrator privileges, so `tryCreateSymlink` gracefully skips (`EACCES`/`EPERM` → early return).

### Regression Test Coverage

**3 new tests, all cross-platform (`.runIf(win32)` gates removed)**:

| Test                                                             | Scenario                                  | Platforms                                                       |
| ---------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `redirects bare date filename to memory/ subdirectory on ENOENT` | Phase 1 ENOENT → Phase 2 redirect         | Linux / macOS / Windows                                         |
| `blocks symlink escape through synthesized memory/ retry path`   | symlink escape → assertSandboxPath blocks | Linux / macOS / Windows (DevMode) / restricted CI adaptive skip |
| `reads root-level date file without triggering memory/ redirect` | root-level file takes priority            | Linux / macOS / Windows                                         |

```
$ pnpm tsgo
# passed clean
```

### Path Normalization

| Input                  | Output                 | Status        |
| ---------------------- | ---------------------- | ------------- |
| `memory/2026-06-12.md` | `memory/2026-06-12.md` | unchanged     |
| `2026-06-12.md`        | `memory/2026-06-12.md` | corrected     |
| `./2026-06-12.md`      | `memory/2026-06-12.md` | corrected     |
| `regular-file.md`      | `undefined`            | not triggered |

### State & Compatibility Note

1. **No persistent serialized session schema changes.** Only runtime path normalization logic is modified; the structure of serialized state remains unchanged. No database/session migration required. The `workspaceRoot` field in `OpenClawReadToolOptions` is runtime-only and never serialized.

2. **Behavior change signoff**: Bare-date `YYYY-MM-DD.md` reads that previously failed with ENOENT will now automatically fall back to `memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md`. This fallback is scoped **strictly** to daily memory date filenames matching `/^(?:memory\/)?\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}\.md$/` and will not affect arbitrary user file reads. Non-matching paths continue to fail with ENOENT as before.

3. **Merge risk assessment**: `merge-risk: session-state` — cleared. The change introduces a new optional `workspaceRoot` field in `OpenClawReadToolOptions` (line 66) and an `executeReadPage` parameter (line 289), both runtime-only. No serialized config, stored session data, or database schema is altered. The `workspaceRoot` injection in `agent-tools.ts:772` is gated on `workspaceOnly`, which is already a runtime config flag. Existing session compatibility is preserved via the fallback chain: `workspaceRoot ? assertSandboxPath : no validation`.

## Root Cause

`DAILY_MEMORY_PATH_RE` at `agent-tools.read.ts:78` was `/^memory\/\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}\.md$/` — only recognized paths with the `memory/` prefix. When the model sent a bare `YYYY-MM-DD.md`, `normalizeDailyMemoryReadPath` returned `undefined`, the read resolved to `workspace-root/YYYY-MM-DD.md` (ENOENT) instead of `workspace-root/memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md`.

Security gap: The ENOENT retry called `params.base.execute` directly with the synthesized `memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md` path, completely bypassing the outer workspace path guard.


Files changed: `src/agents/agent-tools.read.ts` (+45/-8), `src/agents/agent-tools.ts` (+4/-0), `src/agents/agent-tools.workspace-paths.test.ts` (+98/-3).

target.title: fix(security): avoid false-positive secret findings when config uses env references
target.body:
<!--
Closes #62438
-->

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where `openclaw security audit` reported `config.secrets.gateway_password_in_config` (warn) and `config.secrets.hooks_token_in_config` (info) for values that correctly use the documented `${ENV_VAR}` substitution syntax.

## Why This Change Was Made

The secrets-in-config collector was checking the resolved runtime config, where `${...}` placeholders had already been substituted by the config loader. `ConfigFileSnapshot.sourceConfig` is also post-substitution when env vars are set, so this change reads the pre-substitution `parsed` config snapshot and uses those authored values for the env-ref check. The resolved runtime config is still used to decide whether a secret is active at all, so plaintext literals continue to be flagged.

## User Impact

Users who store gateway passwords and hooks tokens as `${ENV_VAR}` references in the main config file no longer see false-positive audit findings. Plaintext literals in config are still flagged.

## Evidence

- Added regression tests in `src/security/audit-config-basics.test.ts` covering both env-ref suppression and continued plaintext flagging, using an explicit `configSnapshot` with pre-substitution `parsed` values.
- Targeted test runs: `pnpm test src/security/audit-config-basics.test.ts` and `pnpm test src/cli/security-cli.test.ts` pass.
- `pnpm tsgo:core` and `pnpm exec oxfmt --check` pass.
- Isolated script proof:
  - Env-ref case (`${OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PASSWORD}`, `${OPENCLAW_HOOKS_TOKEN}`): `hits: []`
  - Literal plaintext case: both `config.secrets.gateway_password_in_config` and `config.secrets.hooks_token_in_config` are emitted.
- Real CLI proof (built from this branch, `pnpm build`, Node v22.22.0):

  Env-ref config:
  ```json
  {
    \"gateway\": { \"auth\": { \"password\": \"${OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PASSWORD}\" } },
    \"hooks\": { \"enabled\": true, \"token\": \"${OPENCLAW_HOOKS_TOKEN}\" }
  }
  ```

  ```bash
  OPENCLAW_HOME=/tmp/audit-proof-62438/envref node openclaw.mjs security audit --json
  ```

  Filtered stored-secret findings:
  ```json
  {
    \"summary\": { \"critical\": 0, \"warn\": 5, \"info\": 1 },
    \"hits\": []
  }
  ```

  Plaintext config:
  ```json
  {
    \"gateway\": { \"auth\": { \"password\": \"literal-password\" } },
    \"hooks\": { \"enabled\": true, \"token\": \"literal-token\" }
  }
  ```

  ```bash
  OPENCLAW_HOME=/tmp/audit-proof-62438/plaintext node openclaw.mjs security audit --json
  ```

  Filtered stored-secret findings:
  ```json
  {
    \"summary\": { \"critical\": 0, \"warn\": 5, \"info\": 2 },
    \"hits\": [
      {
        \"checkId\": \"config.secrets.gateway_password_in_config\",
        \"severity\": \"warn\",
        \"title\": \"Gateway password is stored in config\",
        \"detail\": \"gateway.auth.password is set in the config file; prefer environment variables for secrets when possible.\",
        \"remediation\": \"Prefer OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PASSWORD (env) and remove gateway.auth.password from disk.\"
      },
      {
        \"checkId\": \"config.secrets.hooks_token_in_config\",
        \"severity\": \"info\",
        \"title\": \"Hooks token is stored in config\",
        \"detail\": \"hooks.token is set in the config file; keep config perms tight and treat it like an API secret.\"
      }
    ]
  }
  ```

target.title: fix(channels): keep preview streaming quiet and non-blocking
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where Telegram or Discord users who configure preview streaming could still get block-message delivery when `agents.defaults.blockStreamingDefault` was enabled.

Also fixes a preview-streaming visibility issue where hidden pre-tool commentary could be captured as normal assistant answer text when a channel was configured for answer previews without visible tool/progress lines.

## Why This Change Was Made

Telegram and Discord now resolve block delivery at the channel boundary: explicit channel block-streaming config still wins, live preview modes suppress inherited global block delivery, and `streaming.mode: "off"` can still inherit the global default.

The reply runner now separates commentary capture from commentary display. Channels can capture pre-tool commentary so it stays out of answer text, but only explicit verbose/progress settings render that commentary to the user. Embedded assistant output also suppresses unphased assistant text that ends in tool use, while preserving explicit final-answer text.

## User Impact

Telegram and Discord preview streaming stay on the intended answer-preview path. Users should see normal answer previews and final replies without extra process/preamble bubbles when tool-progress previews are disabled.

## Evidence

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.subscribe-embedded-agent-session.suppresses-commentary-phase-output.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts` passed: 581 tests.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Autoreview was attempted but blocked by local Codex auth: `token_invalidated` / refresh token revoked.

Privacy: no screenshots, raw chat logs, real event text, account identifiers, chat identifiers, or OCR output are included. Tests use synthetic messages and neutral fixture text only.
