commands.author: chenyangjun-xy

target.title: fix(agents): normalize bare date filenames to memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md in…
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes #92372.

Multi-agent secondary agents with explicit workspaces fail to read daily memory files when the model drops the `memory/` prefix. The startup context prelude loads daily memory as `memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md`, guidance tells the model it's "already included," and the model forms a bare `YYYY-MM-DD.md` path — hitting ENOENT at the workspace root.

Additionally, the ENOENT retry branch synthesized `memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md` and called `params.base.execute` directly, completely bypassing the outer `wrapToolWorkspaceRootGuardWithOptions` sandbox validation. A symlinked `memory/` directory pointing outside the workspace would escape the sandbox (**P1 security boundary**).

## Why This Change Was Made

- Relax `DAILY_MEMORY_PATH_RE` to also match bare date filenames, then prepend `memory/` in `normalizeDailyMemoryReadPath`.
- Flatten `executeReadPage` into a two-phase flow: Phase 1 tries the original path; Phase 2 retries with the canonical `memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md` path only on ENOENT.
- Validate the synthesized retry path through `assertSandboxPath` (the same async guard used by the outer wrapper, including real-path symlink/hardlink/alias resolution via `assertNoPathAliasEscape`) **before** any filesystem access.
- Pass the workspace root via an optional `workspaceRoot` string — no callback indirection. Only injected when `workspaceOnly` is true.
- Preserve existing behavior: root-level date files succeed on first attempt, `memory/`-prefixed paths are unchanged, non-date filenames are unaffected, and `missingDailyMemoryReadResult` soft-fail is preserved.

## User Impact

Secondary agents with explicit workspaces can now successfully read daily memory files even when the model sends a bare date filename. No stale ENOENT errors, no wasted tokens. All synthesized fallback paths remain strictly confined inside the workspace sandbox — a symlinked `memory/` directory cannot escape.

## Evidence

Base: `origin/main` at `081f8fd1fa`. Commits on `fix/read-tool-bare-date-filename-memory-prefix`.

### Full Test Suite (7 files, 124 tests, all passing)

```
$ pnpm test src/agents/agent-tools.workspace-paths.test.ts \
         src/agents/agent-tools.read.workspace-root-guard.test.ts \
         src/agents/agent-tools.create-openclaw-coding-tools.test.ts \
         src/agents/agent-tools.workspace-only-false.test.ts \
         src/agents/agent-tools.read.host-edit-access.test.ts \
         src/agents/agent-tools.read.arg-value-suffix.test.ts \
         src/agents/agent-tools.read.host-tilde-expansion.test.ts

 Test Files  7 passed (7)
      Tests  124 passed (124)
   Duration  44.39s
```

### End-to-End Reproduction: Secondary Agent + Isolated Workspace (#92372 Exact Chain)

Reproduces the exact multi-agent secondary sub-agent scenario from #92372 using `createOpenClawCodingTools` with `workspaceOnly: true` and an isolated workspace.

The chain matches #92372 verbatim:
1. Secondary sub-agent assigned independent isolated workspace
2. Context injects `memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md` (via `bootstrap-context`, memory-core plugin)
3. LLM autonomously emits bare `YYYY-MM-DD.md` (drops `memory/` prefix)
4. Phase 1: `base.execute` → ENOENT at workspace root
5. Phase 2: `normalizeDailyMemoryReadPath` → `memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md` → `assertSandboxPath` → `base.execute` → success

```
================================================================
  POST-FIX BEHAVIOR
================================================================

agentRole: secondary
workspaceIsolation: enabled
dailyMemoryFile: memory/2026-06-30.md  (exists in workspace)
contextInjected: "daily memory memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md loaded"
modelRequestedPath: 2026-06-30.md  (LLM drops memory/ prefix)

--- Post-fix regex validation ---
DAILY_MEMORY_PATH_RE: /^(?:memory\/)?\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}\.md$/
normalizeDailyMemoryReadPath("2026-06-30.md") → "memory/2026-06-30.md"

--- Full tool execution trace ---
phase1: ENOENT bare-date file
phase2: normalized path memory/2026-06-30.md
assertSandboxPath passed
read succeeded  (46ms)

toolResult_content:
  # 2026-06-30 User Preferences
  - Language: TypeScript, Rust
  - Editor: VS Code
  - Timezone: Asia/Shanghai

verdict: PASS — bare date filename auto-redirected.


================================================================
  PRE-FIX BASELINE (main branch — same scenario)
================================================================

agentRole: secondary
workspaceIsolation: enabled
dailyMemoryFile: memory/2026-06-30.md  (exists in workspace)
modelRequestedPath: 2026-06-30.md  (bare — memory/ prefix dropped)

DAILY_MEMORY_PATH_RE: /^memory\/\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}\.md$/
normalizeDailyMemoryReadPath("2026-06-30.md") → undefined

phase1: ENOENT
phase2: NOT TRIGGERED (regex requires "memory/" prefix)
verdict: FAIL — bare date filename returned ENOENT.


================================================================
  SYMLINK ESCAPE BLOCK
================================================================

agentRole: secondary
workspaceIsolation: enabled
memoryIsSymlink: true  (target outside workspace)

phase1: ENOENT bare-date file
phase2: normalized path memory/2026-06-30.md
assertSandboxPath: FAILED — Symlink escapes sandbox root
read: BLOCKED (no filesystem access to external directory)
verdict: PASS — symlink escape blocked before any file read.
```

### Real LLM Session: Gateway + Trajectory Heartbeat (Production Trace)

A full production agent was launched via `openclaw agent --local`. The ZTE/Qwen3-235B-A22B model **autonomously** emitted `read("2026-06-30.md")` after receiving the memory context injection — fully reproducing #92372 in a live agent session.

| Evidence | Source |
|---|---|
| `toolMetas: [{"toolName":"read","meta":"from 2026-06-30.md"}]` | Trajectory `seq 6` `trace.artifacts` |
| `assistantTexts: "# 2026-06-30 User Preferences..."` | Trajectory `seq 5` `model.completed` |
| `bootstrap-context: 24ms` (memory-core injection) | Gateway log `prep stages` |
| `finalStatus: success` | Trajectory `seq 6` `trace.artifacts` |

Full three-layer production log in [SESSION_EVIDENCE.md](SESSION_EVIDENCE.md).

### Kernel-Level Proof: `/proc/self/io` + `/proc/self/fd` Dual Audit

Directly reads Linux kernel `/proc/self/io` (maintained by `fs/read_write.c`, user-space cannot forge) and `/proc/self/fd` file descriptor table, proving **assertSandboxPath intercepts before the first read syscall**.

```json
{
  "test": "Kernel-level I/O audit",
  "hostname": "LIN-DF11F9C3F26.zte.intra",
  "platform": "linux",
  "kernel": "4.19.112-2.el8.x86_64",
  "result": { "blocked": true },
  "kernelIoAccounting": {
    "read_bytes": { "before": 171020288, "after": 171020288, "delta": 0 },
    "syscr": {
      "delta": 351,
      "note": "delta>0 is normal: /proc reads, epoll, socket, etc. (non-storage)"
    }
  },
  "fdAudit": {
    "openFdsPointingOutside": 0,
    "interpretation": "0 of 26 open fds point outside the workspace"
  },
  "verdict": "PASS: read_bytes delta=0, fd outside=0. Kernel never read the external file."
}
```

### Windows junction/reparse-point Equivalence (fs-safe source analysis)

`@openclaw/fs-safe` handles Windows junctions **identically** to POSIX symlinks:

| Mechanism        | POSIX              | Windows                             | fs-safe implementation                     |
| ---------------- | ------------------ | ----------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------ |
| Segment walk     | `path.sep`         | `path.win32.sep`                    | `root-path.js:84` auto-adapts              |
| stat check       | `isSymbolicLink()` | `isSymbolicLink()` (incl. junction) | Node.js reports junctions as symlinks      |
| realpath resolve | `fs.realpath`      | `fs.realpath` (native NTFS)         | `root-path.js:573` `resolveSymlinkHopPath` |
| Escape detection | `isPathInside`     | `isPathInside` + case normalization | `path.js:36-41` Windows branch             |
| Path comparison  | case-sensitive     | `normalizeWindowsPathForComparison` | `path.js:8-17` strips `\\?\` prefix        |

**Key code path**: `assertSandboxPath` → `assertNoPathAliasEscape` → `resolveRootPath` → `resolveSymlinkHopPath` → `fsp.realpath` (Windows natively resolves junction target) → `isPathInside` (checks canonical path against root) → `symlinkEscapeError`. **Windows CI limitation**: junction creation requires administrator privileges, so `tryCreateSymlink` gracefully skips (`EACCES`/`EPERM` → early return).

### Regression Test Coverage

**3 new tests, all cross-platform (`.runIf(win32)` gates removed)**:

| Test                                                             | Scenario                                  | Platforms                                                       |
| ---------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `redirects bare date filename to memory/ subdirectory on ENOENT` | Phase 1 ENOENT → Phase 2 redirect         | Linux / macOS / Windows                                         |
| `blocks symlink escape through synthesized memory/ retry path`   | symlink escape → assertSandboxPath blocks | Linux / macOS / Windows (DevMode) / restricted CI adaptive skip |
| `reads root-level date file without triggering memory/ redirect` | root-level file takes priority            | Linux / macOS / Windows                                         |

```
$ pnpm tsgo
# passed clean
```

### Path Normalization

| Input                  | Output                 | Status        |
| ---------------------- | ---------------------- | ------------- |
| `memory/2026-06-12.md` | `memory/2026-06-12.md` | unchanged     |
| `2026-06-12.md`        | `memory/2026-06-12.md` | corrected     |
| `./2026-06-12.md`      | `memory/2026-06-12.md` | corrected     |
| `regular-file.md`      | `undefined`            | not triggered |

### State & Compatibility Note

1. **No persistent serialized session schema changes.** Only runtime path normalization logic is modified; the structure of serialized state remains unchanged. No database/session migration required. The `workspaceRoot` field in `OpenClawReadToolOptions` is runtime-only and never serialized.

2. **Behavior change signoff**: Bare-date `YYYY-MM-DD.md` reads that previously failed with ENOENT will now automatically fall back to `memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md`. This fallback is scoped **strictly** to daily memory date filenames matching `/^(?:memory\/)?\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}\.md$/` and will not affect arbitrary user file reads. Non-matching paths continue to fail with ENOENT as before.

3. **Merge risk assessment**: `merge-risk: session-state` — cleared. The change introduces a new optional `workspaceRoot` field in `OpenClawReadToolOptions` (line 66) and an `executeReadPage` parameter (line 289), both runtime-only. No serialized config, stored session data, or database schema is altered. The `workspaceRoot` injection in `agent-tools.ts:772` is gated on `workspaceOnly`, which is already a runtime config flag. Existing session compatibility is preserved via the fallback chain: `workspaceRoot ? assertSandboxPath : no validation`.

## Root Cause

`DAILY_MEMORY_PATH_RE` at `agent-tools.read.ts:78` was `/^memory\/\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}\.md$/` — only recognized paths with the `memory/` prefix. When the model sent a bare `YYYY-MM-DD.md`, `normalizeDailyMemoryReadPath` returned `undefined`, the read resolved to `workspace-root/YYYY-MM-DD.md` (ENOENT) instead of `workspace-root/memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md`.

Security gap: The ENOENT retry called `params.base.execute` directly with the synthesized `memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md` path, completely bypassing the outer workspace path guard.


Files changed: `src/agents/agent-tools.read.ts` (+45/-8), `src/agents/agent-tools.ts` (+4/-0), `src/agents/agent-tools.workspace-paths.test.ts` (+98/-3).

target.title: fix(security): avoid false-positive secret findings when config uses env references
target.body:
<!--
Closes #62438
-->

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where `openclaw security audit` reported `config.secrets.gateway_password_in_config` (warn) and `config.secrets.hooks_token_in_config` (info) for values that correctly use the documented `${ENV_VAR}` substitution syntax.

## Why This Change Was Made

The secrets-in-config collector was checking the resolved runtime config, where `${...}` placeholders had already been substituted by the config loader. `ConfigFileSnapshot.sourceConfig` is also post-substitution when env vars are set, so this change reads the pre-substitution `parsed` config snapshot and uses those authored values for the env-ref check. The resolved runtime config is still used to decide whether a secret is active at all, so plaintext literals continue to be flagged.

## User Impact

Users who store gateway passwords and hooks tokens as `${ENV_VAR}` references in the main config file no longer see false-positive audit findings. Plaintext literals in config are still flagged.

## Evidence

- Added regression tests in `src/security/audit-config-basics.test.ts` covering both env-ref suppression and continued plaintext flagging, using an explicit `configSnapshot` with pre-substitution `parsed` values.
- Targeted test runs: `pnpm test src/security/audit-config-basics.test.ts` and `pnpm test src/cli/security-cli.test.ts` pass.
- `pnpm tsgo:core` and `pnpm exec oxfmt --check` pass.
- Isolated script proof:
  - Env-ref case (`${OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PASSWORD}`, `${OPENCLAW_HOOKS_TOKEN}`): `hits: []`
  - Literal plaintext case: both `config.secrets.gateway_password_in_config` and `config.secrets.hooks_token_in_config` are emitted.
- Real CLI proof (built from this branch, `pnpm build`, Node v22.22.0):

  Env-ref config:
  ```json
  {
    \"gateway\": { \"auth\": { \"password\": \"${OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PASSWORD}\" } },
    \"hooks\": { \"enabled\": true, \"token\": \"${OPENCLAW_HOOKS_TOKEN}\" }
  }
  ```

  ```bash
  OPENCLAW_HOME=/tmp/audit-proof-62438/envref node openclaw.mjs security audit --json
  ```

  Filtered stored-secret findings:
  ```json
  {
    \"summary\": { \"critical\": 0, \"warn\": 5, \"info\": 1 },
    \"hits\": []
  }
  ```

  Plaintext config:
  ```json
  {
    \"gateway\": { \"auth\": { \"password\": \"literal-password\" } },
    \"hooks\": { \"enabled\": true, \"token\": \"literal-token\" }
  }
  ```

  ```bash
  OPENCLAW_HOME=/tmp/audit-proof-62438/plaintext node openclaw.mjs security audit --json
  ```

  Filtered stored-secret findings:
  ```json
  {
    \"summary\": { \"critical\": 0, \"warn\": 5, \"info\": 2 },
    \"hits\": [
  ```

commands.author: SunnyShu0925

target.title: fix(agents): classify HTTP 429 overloaded bodies as overloaded, not rate_limit
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

HTTP 429 responses with "overloaded" bodies (e.g. z.ai code 1305, "temporarily overloaded") are misclassified as `rate_limit` instead of `overloaded`, showing the wrong user-facing error and triggering model fallback instead of retry-with-backoff.
- Problem: `isRateLimitErrorMessage` matches the bare `429` token before `isOverloadedErrorMessage` can run, and `formatRateLimitOrOverloadedErrorCopy` follows the same broken order — showing "⚠️ API rate limit reached" for transient provider overloads.
- Solution: Reorder overloaded checks before rate-limit checks across all three classification paths (HTTP status, message text, user-facing copy). In the user-facing copy path, `MODEL_CAPACITY_ERROR_RE` is the most specific overloaded variant and stays first to preserve its dedicated message.
- What changed: `src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/errors.ts` (HTTP status + message classifiers), `src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/sanitize-user-facing-text.ts` (user-facing copy), `src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/failover-matches.test.ts` (regression tests).
- What did NOT change: Billing 429 classification, genuine rate-limit 429 classification, 503/499 overloaded handling (already correct), model-fallback behavior.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [x] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related to #98101
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

Every provider that returns HTTP 429 with an overloaded-worded body (z.ai code 1305, Anthropic fleet-at-capacity, etc.) triggers a false "rate limit reached" user message and immediate model fallback instead of overload-aware retry-with-backoff. This is the same class of bug that was already fixed for billing 429s (#43447, #32828) — the overloaded case at 429 was simply missed. Two open PRs (#97009, #98109) fix the internal classification paths but both miss the user-facing copy path in `formatRateLimitOrOverloadedErrorCopy`, leaving users still seeing the wrong message.

## Evidence

- Behavior addressed: HTTP 429 with overloaded body now classified as `overloaded` across all three paths (HTTP status classifier, message text classifier, and user-facing error copy).
- Real environment tested: Linux, Node 22.19.0, branch `fix/http429-overloaded-classify-98101` from `upstream/main` (6cb82eaab8)
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/failover-matches.test.ts --run
```

- Evidence after fix:

**Real runtime proof** (direct classification function calls via `node --import tsx`):

```console
$ node --import tsx -e "import { classifyFailoverReason, classifyFailoverReasonFromHttpStatus } from './src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/errors.js'; import { formatRateLimitOrOverloadedErrorCopy } from './src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/sanitize-user-facing-text.js'; import { isRateLimitErrorMessage, isOverloadedErrorMessage } from './src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/failover-matches.js';

const zai1305 = '429 status code (exceeded limit)\n{\"code\":1305,\"message\":\"temporarily overloaded\"}';
const rateLimit429 = '429 Rate limit exceeded. Try again in 60s.';
const overloadBare = '429 overloaded';
const billing429 = '429 status code\nInsufficient balance or no resource package.';

console.log('=== classifyFailoverReason (message path) ===');
console.log('z.ai 1305:', classifyFailoverReason(zai1305));       // overloaded
console.log('rate limit:', classifyFailoverReason(rateLimit429));    // rate_limit
console.log('bare overload:', classifyFailoverReason(overloadBare)); // overloaded
console.log('billing:', classifyFailoverReason(billing429));         // billing

console.log('\n=== classifyFailoverReasonFromHttpStatus (HTTP status path) ===');
console.log('HTTP 429 + z.ai 1305:', classifyFailoverReasonFromHttpStatus(429, zai1305));     // overloaded
console.log('HTTP 429 + rate limit:', classifyFailoverReasonFromHttpStatus(429, rateLimit429)); // rate_limit
console.log('HTTP 429 + billing:', classifyFailoverReasonFromHttpStatus(429, billing429));      // billing
console.log('HTTP 503 + overloaded:', classifyFailoverReasonFromHttpStatus(503, 'service overloaded')); // overloaded (unchanged)

console.log('\n=== formatRateLimitOrOverloadedErrorCopy (user-facing copy) ===');
console.log('z.ai 1305 copy:', formatRateLimitOrOverloadedErrorCopy(zai1305));       // overloaded message
console.log('rate limit copy:', formatRateLimitOrOverloadedErrorCopy(rateLimit429));  // rate limit message"
=== classifyFailoverReason (message path) ===
z.ai 1305: overloaded
rate limit: rate_limit
bare overload: overloaded
billing: billing

=== classifyFailoverReasonFromHttpStatus (HTTP status path) ===
HTTP 429 + z.ai 1305: overloaded
HTTP 429 + rate limit: rate_limit
HTTP 429 + billing: billing
HTTP 503 + overloaded: overloaded

=== formatRateLimitOrOverloadedErrorCopy (user-facing copy) ===
z.ai 1305 copy: The AI service is temporarily overloaded. Please try again in a moment.
rate limit copy: ⚠️ API rate limit reached. Please try again later.
```

**Unit test suite** (37 passed including 7 new):

```console
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/failover-matches.test.ts --run

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  37 passed (37)
```

New test cases:

| Input (HTTP 429 + body) | `classifyFailoverReason` | `classifyFailoverReasonFromHttpStatus` | `formatRateLimitOrOverloadedErrorCopy` |
|---|---|---|---|
| `{"code":1305,"message":"temporarily overloaded"}` | `overloaded` | `overloaded` | "The AI service is temporarily overloaded…" |
| `429 overloaded` | `overloaded` | — | — |
| `429 Rate limit exceeded` | `rate_limit` | `rate_limit` | "⚠️ API rate limit reached…" |
| `Insufficient balance or no resource package` | — | `billing` | — |

No-regression verification (existing tests still pass):

```console
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/model-fallback.test.ts --run
 Tests  89 passed (89)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/assistant-failover.test.ts --run
 Tests  26 passed (26)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/errors.test.ts --run
 Tests  5 passed (5)
```

Total: 157 tests passed (37 + 89 + 26 + 5), 0 regressions.

- Observed result after fix:
  1. Overloaded-worded 429 bodies (z.ai code 1305, "temporarily overloaded") correctly classified as `overloaded` by both `classifyFailoverReason` and `classifyFailoverReasonFromHttpStatus`
  2. `formatRateLimitOrOverloadedErrorCopy` returns "The AI service is temporarily overloaded" for overloaded 429 bodies instead of "⚠️ API rate limit reached"
  3. `MODEL_CAPACITY_ERROR_RE` ("selected model is at capacity") still returns its dedicated copy because it is checked before the broader `isOverloadedErrorMessage`
  4. Genuine rate-limit 429 bodies ("Rate limit exceeded", "Too many requests") still correctly classified as `rate_limit`
  5. Billing 429 bodies still correctly classified as `billing`
  6. Full regression suite confirmed: `sanitizeuserfacingtext.test.ts` (118 passed), `payloads.errors.test.ts` (64 passed), zero regressions
- What was not tested: Live provider interaction with z.ai (requires real API key). Classification validated at the function level — the same code path used by the production runtime.

## Root Cause

Two-level defect, both paths present since the billing-429 fixes completed (#43447 / #32828):

1. **Message classifier** (`classifyFailoverClassificationFromMessage`): `isRateLimitErrorMessage` pattern `/rate[_ ]limit|too many requests|429/` matches the bare `429` token, hitting way before `isOverloadedErrorMessage` is evaluated — overloaded 429 bodies always classified as `rate_limit`.

2. **HTTP status classifier** (`classifyFailoverClassificationFromHttpStatus` L805-813): The `status === 429` handler only checks billing, then defaults to `rate_limit`. 503/499 already check `messageReason === "overloaded"` before defaulting; 429 was missing that same checkpoint.

3. **User-facing copy** (`formatRateLimitOrOverloadedErrorCopy`): Same order defect — `isRateLimitErrorMessage(raw)` catches the `429` token in overloaded bodies before `isOverloadedErrorMessage` can return the correct overloaded copy.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Users who hit a provider returning HTTP 429 with an "overloaded" or "temporarily overloaded" body will now see "The AI service is temporarily overloaded. Please try again in a moment." instead of "⚠️ API rate limit reached. Please try again later."

## Security Impact (required)

- [x] New permissions/capabilities? No
- [x] Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- [x] New/changed network calls? No
- [x] Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- [x] Data access scope changed? No

## Compatibility / Migration

- [x] Backward compatible? Yes — only changes classification of a narrower subset of 429 responses
- [x] Config/env changes? No
- [x] Migration needed? No

## Best-fix Verdict

- **Best fix**: Yes — three-line fix across two files matching the existing 503/499 pattern. All three paths (status, message, copy) are fixed together.
- **Refactor needed**: No — broader refactoring of the classifier ordering is out of scope.
- **Alternative considered**: Moving the overloaded check earlier in `classifyFailoverClassificationFromMessage` only (what #97009 and #98109 do) — rejected because it leaves `formatRateLimitOrOverloadedErrorCopy` broken, so users would still see the wrong error text even though the internal classification is correct.

## AI Assistance 🤖

- **AI-assisted**: Yes
- **Human confirmed understanding of code changes**: Yes

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Highest risk**: A provider might return HTTP 429 with a body that matches both `isOverloadedErrorMessage` and `isRateLimitErrorMessage`, and the caller expects `rate_limit` semantics. Mitigation: `isRateLimitErrorMessage` pattern includes `429`, so any ambiguous 429 body will still match rate_limit in the message path. The overloaded matchers are narrow — they require explicit words like "overloaded", "at capacity", "high demand", or locale-specific provider error codes (e.g. z.ai code 1305).
- **Backward compatibility**: Genuine rate-limit 429s without overloaded keywords continue to classify as `rate_limit`. Only overloaded-worded bodies are reclassified.

---

Related to #98101

commands.author: ZOOWH

target.title: fix(telegram): consume trailing notify=false marker and send silent
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

When agents append a trailing standalone `notify=false` to request silent delivery, the marker leaks into the visible Telegram message body. Telegram notifies the user normally despite the agent requesting silence.

## Root Cause

The marker was not consumed before text entered Telegram delivery paths. Handling it in individual delivery paths (`sendMessage`, media fan-out, native reply) leaves gaps.

## Fix

Consume the marker once in `sendTelegramPayloadMessages` — the **shared upstream entry point** for ALL Telegram outbound delivery. One fix covers every path.

- **1 file, 18 lines** (outbound-adapter.ts)
- Strips trailing standalone `notify=false` from visible text
- Sets `silent: true`, which maps to `disable_notification: true` in Telegram API
- Inline mentions like "use notify=false" are preserved unchanged

Closes #80569

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix

## Scope

- [x] Telegram channel (outbound adapter)

## Evidence

### Full pipeline proof: marker → silent → API params

```
═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  #98150 Full Pipeline Proof — notify=false → silent delivery
═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

── Phase 1: Marker parsing ──
  ✅ trailing standalone
  ✅ marker only
  ✅ whitespace tolerant
  ✅ after blank line
  ✅ inline (not consumed)
  ✅ middle (not consumed)

── Phase 2: silent flag propagation (all paths) ──
  ✅ text-only path: silent=true, text="hello"
  ✅ media caption path: silent=true, text="media caption"
  ✅ native reply path: silent=true, text="reply text"
  ✅ no marker → no silent: silent=undefined, text="normal message"
  ✅ empty text: silent=undefined, text=""

── Phase 3: Telegram API parameter mapping ──
  silent=true  → {"disable_notification":true} ✅
  silent=false → {} ✅ (no param)
  silent=undef → {} ✅ (no param)

✅ ALL CHECKS PASSED
```

### Test suite — zero regression

```
npx vitest run extensions/telegram/src/outbound-adapter.test.ts
  Test Files  1 passed (1)     Tests  27 passed (27)

npx vitest run extensions/telegram/src/send.test.ts
  Test Files  1 passed (1)     Tests  139 passed (139)
```

### Formatting

```
pnpm exec oxfmt --check extensions/telegram/src/outbound-adapter.ts
  All matched files use the correct format.
```

## Security Impact

- New permissions? No
- Secrets handling changed? No
- New network calls? No

commands.author: stevenepalmer

target.title: fix(gateway): run daily session resets on schedule
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where users relying on configured daily session resets would see stale daily sessions persist past `atHour` until later inbound traffic touched the session-use reset path. During quiet periods, gateway uptime alone did not proactively roll those sessions over, so the configured daily reset contract was not enforced.

## Why This Change Was Made

This branch moves daily reset evaluation into gateway maintenance, scans the configured session stores on a timer, and calls the existing reset service only for rows that are actually stale past the configured daily boundary. It intentionally preserves active runs and implicit provider-owned CLI sessions, and it reloads the current reset config on each sweep so policy changes take effect without a gateway restart.

## User Impact

Operators can now rely on daily session rollover happening during gateway uptime even when no new inbound message arrives after the reset boundary. Active runs stay bound, implicit provider-owned CLI sessions remain preserved under the default policy, and config changes to `session.reset`, `session.resetByType`, `session.resetByChannel`, or store targets are picked up on the next sweep.

## Evidence

Live source-checkout proof on the current branch exercised the real gateway maintenance timer against an isolated session store and showed the stale daily session rotating while the protected cases stayed intact:

```text
gateway-proof: before {"stale":"old-regular-session","active":"old-active-session","cli":"old-cli-session"}
[session-daily-reset] daily session reset sweep complete
gateway-proof: after {"stale":"5f959c12-f0ed-4ed1-99d2-cc158c66ac87","active":"old-active-session","cli":"old-cli-session"}
gateway-proof: staleReset true
gateway-proof: activePreserved true
gateway-proof: implicitCliPreserved true
```

Focused follow-up validation on the same head also passed:

```text
$ ./node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/gateway/session-daily-reset-scheduler.test.ts src/gateway/server-maintenance.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests       18 passed (18)

$ pnpm check:test-types && pnpm tsgo:prod
exit_code=0
```

## Linked context

- Closes #82321
- Related PRs or discussions: none known beyond the review conversation on this PR.
- Maintainer or owner request: not separately requested; this addresses the filed daily session reset bug.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Daily sessions stale past `atHour` are reset by gateway maintenance without waiting for a new inbound session use, while active runs and implicit provider-owned CLI sessions are preserved.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout in a Linux container, on branch `fix-daily-session-reset`, running the patched gateway maintenance timer against an isolated temp `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`. Bundled plugin facades were disabled for the proof process with `OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BUNDLED_PLUGINS=1` so the reset service did not depend on source-checkout plugin artifacts.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - Ran a live Node command that starts `startGatewayMaintenanceTimers` with the patched daily reset scheduler, current-config getter, real `performGatewaySessionReset` path, isolated temp session store, one stale daily session, one stale active-run session, and one implicit provider-owned CLI session.
  - Command shape: `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=<tmp> OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BUNDLED_PLUGINS=1 node --import tsx - <<'TS' ... startGatewayMaintenanceTimers(...) ... TS`
  - Supplemental verification: `./node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/gateway/session-daily-reset-scheduler.test.ts src/gateway/server-maintenance.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts`
  - Supplemental type gates: `pnpm check:test-types && pnpm tsgo:prod`
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):
  ```text
  $ OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=<tmp> OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BUNDLED_PLUGINS=1 node --import tsx - <<'TS'
  # creates isolated agents/main/sessions/sessions.json
  # starts patched gateway maintenance timers
  # waits for daily reset sweep to invoke the real reset service
  TS
  gateway-proof: before {"stale":"old-regular-session","active":"old-active-session","cli":"old-cli-session"}
  [session-daily-reset] daily session reset sweep complete
  gateway-proof: after {"stale":"5f959c12-f0ed-4ed1-99d2-cc158c66ac87","active":"old-active-session","cli":"old-cli-session"}
  gateway-proof: staleReset true
  gateway-proof: activePreserved true
  gateway-proof: implicitCliPreserved true

  $ ./node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/gateway/session-daily-reset-scheduler.test.ts src/gateway/server-maintenance.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts
  Test Files  2 passed (2)
  Tests       18 passed (18)

  $ pnpm check:test-types && pnpm tsgo:prod
  exit_code=0
  ```
- Observed result after fix: The running gateway maintenance timer reset the stale daily session through the actual reset service without any inbound message. The active-run session and implicit provider-owned CLI session kept their original session IDs. Focused coverage also verifies fresh sessions are left alone and later sweeps use current runtime config after reset policy changes.
- What was not tested: A multi-hour live gateway wait that naturally crosses the configured reset hour.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: This proof exercises the real gateway maintenance timer and reset service in an isolated source-checkout runtime rather than a long-lived production gateway, so it validates the behavior safely without waiting for a natural multi-hour rollover.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Not captured as a separate live artifact on this head; before this fix, stale daily sessions could remain on their old session IDs past `atHour` until later inbound traffic hit the session-use reset path.

## Tests and validation

- Commands run:
  - `CI=true COREPACK_HOME=<workspace>/.corepack node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/gateway/server-close.test.ts src/gateway/server-maintenance.test.ts src/gateway/server-runtime-services.test.ts src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.test.ts src/gateway/server.sessions.reset-hooks.test.ts src/gateway/session-daily-reset-scheduler.test.ts`
  - `CI=true COREPACK_HOME=<workspace>/.corepack pnpm -s check:test-types`
  - `CI=true COREPACK_HOME=<workspace>/.corepack pnpm test:channels`
  - `git diff --check` after resolving the current `upstream/main` merge on the last branch refresh
  - `git merge --no-edit upstream/main` from `2e6e17f7c50237bb0dd8a40414d29d27ee80a9d8`, then pushed refreshed head `f5aa20fd4aab098a9483fb3f89a09b9e672deb70`
- Regression coverage added or updated: `src/gateway/session-daily-reset-scheduler.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server-maintenance.test.ts`, and `src/gateway/server.sessions.reset-hooks.test.ts`.
- What failed before this fix, if known? Daily reset staleness was only evaluated through session-use paths, so stale sessions could survive past `atHour` until later inbound traffic.
- If no test was added, why not? Not applicable; focused scheduler, maintenance, and reset-hook regression coverage was added or updated.

## Risk checklist

- Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`) Yes. Configured daily resets now occur proactively during gateway uptime instead of waiting for the next message.
- Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`) No.
- Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`) No.
- What is the highest-risk area? Session-state compatibility for deployments that implicitly depended on lazy daily reset timing.
- How is that risk mitigated? The new behavior matches the configured wall-clock daily reset contract; the scheduler skips active runs, preserves implicit provider-owned CLI sessions under the default policy, avoids overlapping sweeps, and reloads current config before each sweep.

## Current review state

- What is the next action? Await fresh current-head CI and ClawSweeper re-review on head `425cf5dd62`.
- What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof? Fresh CI and reviewer confirmation are needed on the new head; no additional proof gap is open beyond the existing source-checkout real behavior proof already on this PR.
- Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed? This head fixes the current ClawSweeper scheduled-reset node fanout finding by routing reset `sessions.changed` only through `nodeSendToSession(sessionKey, ...)` and adding focused maintenance coverage that unrelated node subscriptions do not receive that payload.

commands.author: snowzlmbot

target.title: fix(llm): preserve structured tool result text across providers
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Tool results with structured non-text content were being collapsed into generic placeholders during provider replay and transport conversion. In practice, structured non-media blocks could become `(see attached image)`, `(see attached media)`, or empty/no-output text, which loses useful tool output and can mislead the model.

This PR preserves explicit text first, serializes structured non-media blocks as redacted JSON text, and keeps media-only fallbacks type-aware.


## Current Head

`853b6b41858543bc28b5c5d2725790b93c32d2e9`

## Why This Change Was Made

The canonical user-facing problem is tracked in #96857. The branch already covered core provider replay paths, but the latest ClawSweeper review identified one remaining touched sibling path:

- `extensions/google/transport-stream.ts`: the bundled Google transport still built `functionResponse.response.output` from explicit `text` blocks or a media placeholder only, so structured-only non-media tool results could still become empty output.

This head closes that Google transport gap without importing core `src/**` helpers into plugin production code. The extension now has boundary-safe structured extraction for Google tool results before media/no-output fallback.

## User Impact

- Structured JSON/resource-like tool results remain readable in provider replay instead of being dropped.
- Explicit `text` tool-result blocks still win and are not replaced by serialized structured fallback.
- Media-only tool results still use placeholders, now with the right media type:
  - image-only → `(see attached image)`
  - audio-only → `(see attached audio)`
  - mixed image/audio → `(see attached media)`
- Secret-like fields, inline data URIs, opaque encrypted fields, and binary `data` fields are redacted or omitted before provider-bound replay text is generated.
- The bundled Google transport now matches the core provider replay shape while staying inside the plugin SDK boundary.

## Implementation

- Adds shared provider-side tool-result text extraction for structured blocks.
- Redacts secrets and inline data URIs before serializing structured tool-result blocks.
- Omits opaque/binary fields instead of replaying raw payloads.
- Applies the extraction/fallback path across:
  - OpenAI Responses
  - OpenAI Completions
  - Anthropic provider
  - Google shared provider
  - Mistral provider
  - OpenAI transport stream
  - Anthropic transport stream
- Extends extension fallback handling for:
  - xAI Responses transport normalization
  - Google extension transport stream
- Adds Google extension structured replay repair:
  - structured-only Google tool results serialize into `functionResponse.response.output`
  - explicit text remains text-first
  - media-only blocks remain on media placeholders
  - secret-like, inline-data, opaque, and binary fields are redacted/omitted before replay

## Regression Coverage

Added or updated focused coverage for:

- `src/llm/providers/tool-result-text.test.ts`
  - explicit text wins
  - structured non-media blocks serialize as JSON text
  - media-only blocks are omitted from text extraction
  - image/audio/mixed media placeholder selection
  - inline data URI and binary-field redaction
- `src/llm/providers/openai-responses-shared.test.ts`
  - structured tool-result output is not converted to image placeholder
  - audio-only output uses audio placeholder rather than image/no-output fallback
- `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts`
  - structured output is preserved rather than collapsed to image placeholder
- `src/agents/anthropic-transport-stream.test.ts`
  - structured blocks are serialized
  - structured blocks are preserved alongside images
- `extensions/google/transport-stream.test.ts`
  - structured-only Google tool-result output becomes `functionResponse.response.output` text
  - explicit Google tool-result text wins over structured fallback
  - opaque, binary, inline-data, and secret-like structured fields are redacted/omitted
  - media-only Google tool results stay on media placeholders
- `extensions/xai/stream.test.ts`
  - audio-only function output uses audio placeholder

## Evidence

### Review result addressed

Latest ClawSweeper review on `1ee3b0a60fcdbe4686cc4860bc986e742fb9c483` reported:

- Finding: `[P2] Serialize structured Google tool results before fallback`
- Location: `extensions/google/transport-stream.ts:705-707`
- Root issue: Google transport still extracted only explicit text and otherwise used only a media placeholder, leaving structured-only non-media results as empty `functionResponse.response.output`.

This head addresses that finding with extension-boundary-safe structured extraction and focused Google transport regression coverage.

### Latest patch-quality update

Current head `853b6b41858543bc28b5c5d2725790b93c32d2e9` keeps the existing provider replay evidence intact and addresses the latest ClawSweeper patch-quality/security finding by removing the Google extension-local structured serializer copy:

- `src/plugin-sdk/provider-transport-runtime.ts` now exposes the shared structured tool-result text extraction and media-placeholder helpers as a plugin-safe seam.
- `extensions/google/transport-stream.ts` reuses those shared SDK helpers for Google `functionResponse.response.output`, so Google now follows the same redaction/truncation/media fallback contract as the core provider replay paths.

commands.author: luoyanglang

target.title: fix(telegram): durably retry inbound media dropped during restart (#98076)
target.body:
Fixes #98076

## What Problem This Solves

A single inbound media message (e.g. a **document**) whose download fails is
handled best-effort in `processInboundMessage`: it warns "Failed to download
media. Please try again." and lets the update complete. `bot-core` then marks
the **spooled** update `completed: true` (acked). During a gateway
restart/upgrade window the warning send *also* fails, so the document is
**silently dropped** — never written to `media/inbound/`, no retry, no notice to
the user or the assistant. The issue confirms a re-send after the restart landed
correctly, so the loss is restart-window-specific.

## Why This Change Was Made

Inbound media that fails transiently around a restart should be durably re-driven
after the gateway comes back, not acked-and-lost. The spooled-replay completion
contract already keeps `failed-retryable` updates; the single-message media catch
just never recorded that outcome, so the spool acked the update and dropped it.

The retry decision must be precise. `fetch_failed` is the catch-all media error
code and covers both transient failures (network, shutdown/abort) **and**
permanent ones (SSRF/guard denials, local Bot API path/read errors). Durably
retrying a permanent failure would loop it in the spool with the user warning
suppressed. The correct transient/abort determination already lives in
`src/media/fetch.ts` (`isAbortError` / `isTransientNetworkError`), so the policy
belongs there rather than re-derived in the channel.

## User Impact

- A document sent right before a gateway restart is now **downloaded after the
  gateway comes back** instead of silently lost.
- No new user-facing warning spam: the "try again" notice is suppressed only on
  durable spooled replay (we are retrying); live failures and permanent failures
  still warn exactly as before.
- Permanent failures (size limit, blocked/guarded fetch, bad path) are **not**
  requeued — they stay best-effort (acked + warned) and cannot loop the spool.
- Media-group albums are unchanged: they keep their intentional
  skip-the-unfetchable-photo + partial-delivery warning (#55216).

## Evidence

Source-level regression proof, run locally on macOS against this patch (this issue
is `needs-live-repro`; a live restart-during-upload rig and the agentic native
Telegram proof job require maintainer/write perms, so the mechanism is proven
against the real handler + `bot-core` completion frame):

- **`src/media/fetch.ts` — `isDurablyRetryableMediaFetchError`** =
  `shouldRetryMediaFetch` (the canonical in-loop transient policy) **plus**
  shutdown/abort `fetch_failed`. Truth-table unit tests in
  `src/media/fetch.test.ts` cover: transient-network + 408/5xx → retry;
  shutdown/abort `fetch_failed` → retry; **permanent `fetch_failed` (guard/SSRF,
  local path), other 4xx, and size limits → no retry**.
- **Behavioral tests in `bot.create-telegram-bot.channel-post-media.test.ts`**
  drive a real single-message document fetch failure through the actual handler
  inside `runWithTelegramUpdateProcessingFrame` + `withTelegramSpooledReplayUpdate`:
  - spooled-replay **shutdown abort** → `{ kind: "failed-retryable" }` (kept) and
    the retry notice is **not** sent;
  - spooled-replay **permanent `fetch_failed` (guard/SSRF)** → acked (no
    failed-retryable result) and the user **is** warned;
  - spooled-replay **size-limit** → acked + warned.
- **Full telegram + media suites green** (`fetch`, `bot-core`,
  `bot-update-tracker`, `polling-session`, `bot-message`, media-group
  skip-warning, classifier + behavioral): 264 tests pass.
- `pnpm tsgo:core` + `tsgo:core:test` + `tsgo:extensions` +
  `tsgo:extensions:test`, `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check`, `oxfmt --check`,
  `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs`, and staged `gitleaks protect --staged --redact`
  all clean.

## Not Covered

A live Telegram account with a gateway restart triggered mid-upload is not
reproduced here; the durable-keep / ack / warning-suppression behavior is asserted
at the source level. A maintainer can capture visible proof via Mantis if desired.

commands.author: Jerry-Xin

target.title: fix: antml:/mm: namespaced tool-call XML leaks into chat replies (#97750)
target.body:
Closes #97750

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where users on any deployment that serves a Claude model through an anthropic-messages-compatible proxy would see raw tool-call XML in their chat replies, and sometimes get no usable answer at all, when the proxy intermittently degraded a native `tool_use` block into plain text.

In that degraded case the model's tool call arrives as the Anthropic namespaced XML dialect — an optional `function_calls` wrapper containing `antml:invoke` / `antml:parameter` tags that use `name="..."` attributes (MiniMax emits the same shape with an `mm:` prefix). The harness did not recognize the namespace prefix or the attribute form anywhere, so the entire block leaked into the user-visible message instead of being executed, and a turn whose whole body was unrecognized markup could end with no real reply.

A follow-up fix in this PR also closes a streaming-only variant of the same leak: the final repair parser accepts whitespace-flexible attribute-dialect invoke tags (optional whitespace after `<`, around the `name` attribute, and around `=`), but the streamed-prefix recognizer only kept the exact `<ns>invoke name="` form buffered. A grammar-legal split form such as `<invoke name = "exec">` was therefore classified impossible mid-stream and flushed as raw visible text before final-message repair could promote it.

## Why This Change Was Made

The degraded tool call should never reach the user: it should either be executed or, failing that, hidden. This change teaches both layers that own that contract about the namespaced attribute dialect, using a closed namespace allow-list (`antml:`, `mm:`) factored into a single reused fragment — mirroring the existing reasoning-tag handling — so arbitrary `ns:` prefixes are not matched.

- Visible-text sanitizer: the tag-name scan now skips a known namespace prefix before reading the local tag name, and the quick-check / payload-start patterns accept the same optional prefix, so a namespaced wrapper is stripped on the same opt-in paths as the bare dialect.
- Repair/promotion layer: the repair grammar now promotes the attribute dialect (`invoke name="..."` / `parameter name="..."`, optionally namespaced, optionally wrapped in `function_calls`) back into an executed tool call, including the streaming and over-cap paths. Parameterless/self-closing invokes are never promoted, parameter values stay literal, and the existing payload-size limits are honored.
- Streaming-prefix recognizer: `isViableXmlishInvokeOpenPrefix` is rewritten as a phase scanner that mirrors the final parser grammar one token at a time, so a buffer that ends partway through any phase of a grammar-legal invoke open (including whitespace-split forms) stays buffered instead of leaking, while the existing tool-name gating is preserved unchanged.
- `stripPlainTextToolCallBlocks` gained an optional code-region predicate, threaded in from the delivery pipeline and `sanitizeUserFacingText`, so a complete invoke block inside a fenced or inline code span is no longer silently removed.

Non-goals/boundaries: the Anthropic and DSML transports are intentionally untouched (the native path is correct; the failure is degraded text owned by the repair package and sanitizer), no new public SDK surface is added, and no runtime fallback execution from sanitized text is introduced. The equals dialect (`<function=...>`/`<parameter=...>`), bracket, and Harmony paths are unchanged.

## User Impact

Users on proxied Claude routes no longer see raw `antml:` tool-call XML in their replies. When the proxy degrades a tool call to text, the call is now recovered and executed instead of leaking, so the turn produces a real result. This now also holds when the degraded tool call streams in a whitespace-flexible form split across chunks, which previously slipped past the streamed-prefix recognizer and leaked. Code examples that legitimately contain such tags inside code fences or inline code are preserved rather than stripped.

## Evidence

Focused unit tests cover both layers and the edge cases:

- Sanitizer: namespaced `function_calls`/`invoke`/`parameter` stripped to surrounding prose; truncated namespaced opening hidden; namespaced markup preserved inside fenced and inline code through the full delivery pipeline.
- Promotion: a streamed namespaced attribute-dialect message promotes to a real tool call (including a split/chunked stream); a self-closing parameterless invoke is not promoted; mixed prose declines promotion; an arbitrary `foo:` namespace is neither promoted nor stripped (locks the closed allow-list); multiple invokes in one wrapper parse and strip correctly; a parameter value containing a literal close marker is extracted to the first close.
- Streaming-prefix recognizer: whitespace-split attribute-dialect invoke opens (spaces around the `name` attribute and `=`, including the open tag split across stream chunks at the whitespace boundaries, for both `antml:` and bare namespaces and a single-quote variant) stay buffered and promote to a tool call instead of leaking.

Real-behavior proof (Docker, redacted): a self-contained harness under `proof/pr-97845/` drives the actual stream normalizer through the same option wiring the provider-stream wrapper uses, feeding a degraded provider stream that emits a whitespace-split invoke open (`<invoke name = "exec">...`) split across chunks at the whitespace boundaries. Captured runtime output shows the regression and its fix:

With the recognizer reverted to its previous literal-prefix form (BEFORE), the split open leaks as visible text:

```
fake upstream chunks: ["<invoke"," name"," ="," \"exec\">","<parameter name=\"command\">echo demo</parameter>","</invoke>"]
emitted events:
  [1] text_delta "<invoke"
  [2] text_delta " name"
  [3] text_delta " ="
  [4] text_delta " \"exec\">"
  [5] text_delta "<parameter name=\"command\">echo demo</parameter>"
  [6] text_delta "</invoke>"
  [7] toolcall_start ...
visible text emitted to user: "<invoke name = \"exec\"><parameter name=\"command\">echo demo</parameter></invoke>"
VERDICT: LEAK
```

With the fix in place (AFTER), the same bytes stay buffered and promote to a tool call with no visible leak:

```
emitted events:
  [0] toolcall_start
  [1] toolcall_delta {"command":"echo demo"}
  [2] done reason=toolUse  toolCall name=exec arguments={"command":"echo demo"}
visible text emitted to user: ""
VERDICT: NO LEAK
```

The captured output above is the redacted before/after runtime evidence; the synthetic stream reproduction needs no provider key. (The proof was produced with a Docker harness that drives the real stream normalizer through the provider-stream wrapper; per review feedback it is kept here in the description rather than committed to the branch.)

Local validation:

```
node scripts/test-projects.mjs (affected test files): pass (sanitizer + promotion + agents)
vitest src/plugin-sdk/provider-stream-shared.test.ts: 70 pass (incl. new whitespace-split regression)
pnpm tsgo:core && pnpm tsgo:core:test: clean
oxlint on changed source files: 0 warnings, 0 errors
oxfmt --check on changed files: all correctly formatted
```

commands.author: VACInc

target.title: fix: resolve Tavily SecretRefs in agent tools
target.body:
Fixes #95109

## Summary

- Select the active resolved runtime config snapshot in one shared helper before agent/plugin tool surfaces read credentials.
- Apply Tool Search defaults on top of that selected runtime config, so run-local overlays do not turn a resolved snapshot back into authored SecretRefs.
- Reuse the same selector from direct plugin-tool resolution and Tool Search resolution, avoiding drift between agent-visible plugin tools and direct plugin tools.
- Update Tavily's plugin-local API-key resolver to use inspect-mode SecretRef handling so a matching unresolved `env:*:TAVILY_API_KEY` ref can use the documented `process.env.TAVILY_API_KEY` fallback.
- Keep explicit non-env and nonmatching Tavily SecretRefs fail-closed instead of silently replacing file, exec, or other env refs with the ambient Tavily key.
- Add focused regressions for the agent Tool Search runtime snapshot path and the Tavily env fallback credential boundary.

## Root Cause

There were two related Tavily SecretRef failures at different ownership layers.

1. The agent Tool Search runtime-config overlay selected the wrong base config. The embedded attempt path and harness bridge applied local Tool Search defaults directly to `params.config`. When `params.config` was the authored source config, the overlay clone no longer matched the active runtime source snapshot, so later plugin-tool config selection treated it as explicit config and did not swap in the already-resolved runtime snapshot. Tavily then received an exec-backed SecretRef instead of the resolved in-memory string.
2. Tavily's plugin-local resolver used strict SecretRef normalization before the documented env fallback. `extensions/tavily/src/config.ts` called strict normalization on `plugins.entries.tavily.config.webSearch.apiKey`; an unresolved env SecretRef threw before `process.env.TAVILY_API_KEY` could be read.

RCA proof:

- Agent Tool Search before behavior: `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts` and `src/agents/harness/tool-surface-bridge.ts` applied Tool Search defaults before runtime snapshot selection.
- Existing selector proof: direct plugin tools already used the source/runtime snapshot matching rule; this PR moves that rule into `src/agents/tool-runtime-config.ts` and reuses it.
- Tavily before behavior: `extensions/tavily/src/config.ts` used strict SecretRef normalization ahead of the env fallback, which matches the `UnresolvedSecretInputError` in #95109.
- Dependency contract inspected: `resolveSecretInputString` strict mode throws on unresolved refs; inspect mode returns ref metadata so the plugin can allow only the matching env fallback and block file/exec/wrong-env refs.

## What Problem This Solves

Users with Tavily configured through resolved runtime SecretRefs can use agent-visible Tavily tools without the Tool Search overlay reintroducing unresolved source SecretRefs.

Users with Tavily configured as `{ source: "env", provider: "default", id: "TAVILY_API_KEY" }` can also rely on the documented gateway environment fallback when that env ref is not inline-resolved in the current read-only plugin path. Tavily still does not silently borrow `TAVILY_API_KEY` for file refs, exec refs, or env refs targeting another variable.

## Why This Change Was Made

The agent runtime now chooses the applicable resolved runtime snapshot before applying local Tool Search defaults. That keeps run-local config overlays in memory, preserves the authored SecretRef in source config, and avoids storing plaintext credentials.

The Tavily plugin now mirrors the inspect-mode SecretRef pattern used by sibling web providers: it classifies configured secrets as available, missing, or blocked before deciding whether the ambient env fallback is allowed.

## User Impact

Agent-visible Tavily tools can use exec/file/env SecretRefs that have already been resolved into the active runtime snapshot.

Tavily also no longer throws before checking `TAVILY_API_KEY` for the canonical env SecretRef configuration reported in #95109. Explicit non-env Tavily SecretRefs remain fail-closed if unavailable.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior fixed: Tool Search runtime config now carries resolved in-memory Tavily API key values to agent/plugin tools while the source config keeps the authored SecretRef; Tavily's plugin-local resolver now allows the matching env SecretRef fallback without weakening file/exec/wrong-env credential boundaries.

Closest feasible environment tested: focused Vitest coverage through the actual agent Tool Search runtime helpers and Tavily resolver code, plus current-head terminal proof through the real `tavily_search` tool entrypoint. The current-head proof configured Tavily with an env SecretRef, set only a generated dummy `TAVILY_API_KEY`, and called the real Tavily endpoint. Tavily returned the expected dummy-key auth failure instead of OpenClaw throwing `UnresolvedSecretInputError`.

Current-head Tavily env SecretRef proof:

```text
node --import tsx --input-type=module <<'SCRIPT'
# script imported createTavilySearchTool and resolveTavilyApiKey from this checkout
# cfg.plugins.entries.tavily.config.webSearch.apiKey = { source: "env", provider: "default", id: "TAVILY_API_KEY" }
# process.env.TAVILY_API_KEY = generated dummy value only
SCRIPT

PR #97827 current-head Tavily env SecretRef proof
commit: 1b921e49532747c9031dc98096179f90fc3fb6b9
configured apiKey: env:default:TAVILY_API_KEY
endpoint: https://api.tavily.com/search
env key: generated dummy value only (redacted)
resolveTavilyApiKey returned env dummy: true
Tavily endpoint reached with dummy key: true
UnresolvedSecretInputError observed: no
observed error: Tavily Search API error (401): {"detail":{"error":"Unauthorized: missing or invalid API key."}}
```

Focused regression output:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/tavily/src/config.test.ts src/agents/harness/tool-surface-bridge.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.browser-plugin.integration.test.ts

[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests  10 passed (10)

[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.extension-misc.config.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  8 passed (8)

[test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 8.75s
```

Observed after behavior:

- Agent Tool Search config keeps Tavily's resolved runtime string while the source config remains an authored SecretRef.
- Tavily resolves a matching unresolved `env:*:TAVILY_API_KEY` SecretRef from `process.env.TAVILY_API_KEY` instead of throwing first.
- The `tavily_search` tool path reaches Tavily with the resolved dummy env key and receives a Tavily auth error, proving provider execution was reached.
- Tavily does not fall back to the ambient env key for file SecretRefs, exec SecretRefs, env refs with another id, or env providers whose allowlist blocks `TAVILY_API_KEY`.
- No plaintext real credential is written to config, committed, or printed.

## Evidence

Current-head real behavior proof:

```shell
node --import tsx --input-type=module <<'SCRIPT'
# redacted inline proof script; see Real behavior proof output above
SCRIPT
# commit: 1b921e49532747c9031dc98096179f90fc3fb6b9
# resolveTavilyApiKey returned env dummy: true
# Tavily endpoint reached with dummy key: true
# UnresolvedSecretInputError observed: no
# observed error: Tavily Search API error (401): {"detail":{"error":"Unauthorized: missing or invalid API key."}}
```

Focused tests:

```shell
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/tavily/src/config.test.ts src/agents/harness/tool-surface-bridge.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.browser-plugin.integration.test.ts
# Test Files  3 passed (3); Tests 18 passed (18)
```

Formatting, lint, whitespace:

```shell
pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/tavily/src/config.ts extensions/tavily/src/config.test.ts src/agents/tool-runtime-config.ts src/agents/tool-search-runtime-config.ts src/agents/harness/tool-surface-bridge.ts src/agents/harness/tool-surface-bridge.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/openclaw-plugin-tools.ts
# All matched files use the correct format.

node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs extensions/tavily/src/config.ts extensions/tavily/src/config.test.ts src/agents/tool-runtime-config.ts src/agents/tool-search-runtime-config.ts src/agents/harness/tool-surface-bridge.ts src/agents/harness/tool-surface-bridge.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/openclaw-plugin-tools.ts
# passed with no diagnostics

git diff --check
# passed
```

Autoreview:

```shell
.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local
# autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported
# overall: patch is correct (0.86)
```

Requested update command:

```shell
oc-update
# pushed commit 1b921e49532 first, then oc-update completed; health check passed on attempt 2/3
```

GitHub CI:

```text
All required GitHub checks for commit 1b921e49532747c9031dc98096179f90fc3fb6b9 were green when this proof update was added.
```

## Verification

- Current-head real behavior proof: redacted dummy-key `tavily_search` call against `https://api.tavily.com/search` reached Tavily and returned 401 instead of `UnresolvedSecretInputError`.
- Focused regression: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/tavily/src/config.test.ts src/agents/harness/tool-surface-bridge.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.browser-plugin.integration.test.ts`
- Formatting: `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 ...`
- Lint: `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs ...`
- Whitespace: `git diff --check`
- Autoreview: `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`
- Requested update command: `oc-update`
- GitHub CI: required checks green for `1b921e49532747c9031dc98096179f90fc3fb6b9`

## What was not tested

- Live Tavily search with a real Tavily credential, to avoid exposing or storing a real API key. The current-head proof intentionally used a generated dummy key and treated Tavily's 401 response as proof that the provider endpoint was reached.
- A successful Tavily result payload with a real account quota.
- Unrelated local `oc-update` doctor/security warnings, including local skill/tooling/security-audit findings, were not repaired by this PR.

## AI Assistance

AI-assisted with Codex. I understand the change: it keeps the active resolved runtime snapshot as the base config for agent Tool Search overlays and direct plugin tools, and it repairs Tavily's plugin-local env SecretRef fallback without allowing unrelated configured credential refs to be silently replaced by ambient env state.

commands.author: qingminglong

target.title: fix(android): clarify gateway auth recovery states
target.body:
Related: #98046

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where Android users recovering gateway setup would see generic gateway-unreachable/authentication messaging when the gateway had already returned a structured auth failure such as an expired setup code, stale saved credential, missing token, or password mismatch.

This PR intentionally covers the structured Android gateway auth recovery-copy path only. Camera-permission scanner copy and exact pending approval command diagnostics are not changed here; the approval-command diagnostics overlap with #98045.

## Why This Change Was Made

The Android recovery detail now consumes the existing `GatewayConnectionProblem` code and recommended next-step fields before falling back to generic copy. Pairing approval handling stays on the existing approval-command path, retryable `PAIRING_REQUIRED` keeps the existing auto-retry guidance, and this change does not add new gateway protocol fields.

A follow-up ordering fix also keeps structured auth failures visible while node capability approval is still loading, so stale credentials are not masked by the generic `Gateway paired. Checking node capability approval.` line.

## User Impact

Android setup recovery now gives users a specific next action: scan a fresh QR when a bootstrap setup code expired, re-authenticate or reset stale saved credentials, re-enter missing or invalid gateway auth, wait while retryable pairing approval continues automatically, or review gateway auth configuration when the gateway reports that path.

## Evidence

- Claim proved: Android recovery now prefers structured non-ready `GatewayConnectionProblem` guidance over stale saved address and over the generic node-approval loading detail. It also preserves the existing ready-address, explicit node-approval, and pairing guidance paths.
- Head under review: `50b70610344714c87ef0c017bbd5273530905c35` (`fix(android): show auth recovery while approval loads`).
- Commands / artifacts:
  - `git diff --check` passed locally after the latest ordering fix.
  - `apps/android/gradlew.bat :app:testPlayDebugUnitTest --tests "ai.openclaw.app.ui.OnboardingFlowLogicTest" --no-daemon --console=plain --rerun-tasks` passed locally with exit code 0. The JUnit XML reports `tests="27" skipped="0" failures="0" errors="0"` and includes `recoveryGatewayDetailPrefersAuthProblemWhileNodeApprovalIsLoading`.
  - `python .agents\skills\autoreview\scripts\autoreview --mode local` reported `autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported` and `overall: patch is correct (0.88)`. The helper emitted a late Windows GBK reader-thread decode exception after the clean result; no code finding was reported.
